programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ziva confronts her past when the team is ordered to protect her father, Mossad director Eli David, at a gathering of former heads of NCIS. The agents discover he is being targeted by terrorists who have hijacked a shipment of advanced weapons from the army. Drama, guest starring TJ Ramini and Michael Nouri, with Cote de Pablo and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam meet up with former colleague Mike Renko, who is back in town on the trail of a civilian militia he thinks is responsible for the theft of anti-tank missiles from a Navy base. Their discussion is interrupted by Hetty with footage of a man being strangled at an aquarium - and the victim is identified as a lance corporal who was Renko's inside man at the group

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The unit is called to examine the mysterious death of a sailor serving on a research vessel engaged in a top-secret mission. Drama, starring David McCallum, Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Lauren Holly

programme.title: NCIS

Crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A childhood friend of Gibbs' dead daughter is stalked by a marine who recently returned from Iraq, and asks the agent for help. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy Lieutenant who has been murdered while on his way to a meeting with the President, and must ascertain whether the killing was part of a recent spate of muggings in the area - or a targeted hit to keep information secret. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky works on a case involving his estranged childhood best friend, travelling to London with Special Agent Bishop to interview his pal's family and co-workers. While away, the detective reflects on his own past choices, and examines how they have affected his present life. Alice Krige guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Christmas special from 1989. The Essex girls steel themselves for a lonely Christmas away from their jailbird husbands. Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's heating breaks down, leaving them with no one to turn to - except their man-hungry neighbour Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: John Heaton tells Gina the station faces the threat of closure and the team must pull together to keep Sun Hill on the map. New arrival Sgt Nikki Wright (Gillian Taylforth) is determined to show what she's made of, but fails to impress Roger, who complains to Doug Wright about her, not realising they are a married couple. Tony investigates an armed robbery at a jeweller's and a drug addict's body is discovered in a disused flat. A proposal from Rod Jessop stuns June

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Struggling to deal with her feelings for both Sam and Kieron, Maria consults a clairvoyant. She is shown visions of her life in the past, present and future, which help her come to a decision. Since Faye is the only one offering her any assistance, Donna is missing the nurse's company as she prepares for the Christmas party and begins to doubt their friendship. Sam's bond with his newly discovered son is threatened by his awareness of Kieron's relationship with Maria, but, with everything out in the open, the surgeon realises where his priorities lie

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A birthday meal for Jim turns into a surprise engagement party for Nikki. Lara tells Harry she only sees him as a friend, and back at Holby, a man is admitted after a suffocation attempt by his partner, who can no longer cope with his deteriorating lung cancer. Gwyneth Strong and Tony Slattery guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Dan attempts heroics in a bid to rescue an 11-year-old from a canal boat where he is being held. The abductor turns out to be Rick Harris, whose addict son was found dead the previous day. June's private life gets entangled with public duties when she's called to boyfriend Rod's school to investigate one of his pupils - Kyle Henderson of the troublesome lottery-winning family - and there's a shock in store for her when the headmaster is critically injured in a hit-and-run incident

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat comes down hard on Barry and David's dodgy deals. Ruth is determined to get to the bottom of Mark's problems. Neither Ricky nor David have the time for Bianca she would like

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark is getting into deep water and is feeling more isolated. Grant pulls a fast one, but has not covered his tracks very well. Cindy finds she is out of her depth

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is delighted when Sarah agrees to marry him. When Elizabeth finds out about the proposal, she realises her threat to disinherit him has backfired - and decides to take a different approach. Elsewhere, Anna encounters complications with her pregnancy, and James and Olivia say goodbye to Harry before they leave Ash Park

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A childless woman's bid to adopt is thrown into jeopardy by ill health and it's Sam who has to break the bad news. The burden of caring for baby Joe takes its toll on Tom, who resorts to finding a nanny to share the responsibility, and a recently widowed elderly gent announces to Andrew he feels he has nothing to live for

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The boxer dog Mrs Pumphrey bought to keep Tricky Woo company has a particularly embarrassing problem. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Margaretta Scott

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: In a bid to avoid the arrival of Emma's in-laws, William and Hester go on a rural camping trip, but manage to end up in Paris. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Jane and Diana's strip-club hen night proves to be a real eye-opener, then a double booking with a funeral at the church throws the happy couples' wedding plans into chaos. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth attends the christening of Daisy's granddaughter, and is displeased to learn the child's parents are not married. With some encouragement from Richard, she agrees to keep an open mind, but is later irked when the mother and child go missing. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After seeing a man skiing on a van roof, the pals are intrigued to discover it is Gavin Hinchcliffe, an old friend renowned for his eccentricity. Comedy series, guest starring Bernard Cribbins, with Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Josephine Tewson

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Anna Westin, a troubled childhood friend of Wallander's daughter, visits his house late at night begging for his help - but disappears soon afterwards. Meanwhile, an elderly woman's burned body is found in a shallow grave, and when the detective discovers a connection between Anna and the murder victim, he fears she could be the next target of a dangerous religious fanatic. Linda returns to Ystad to help with the investigation, and has life-changing news for her father. Guest starring Lindsay Duncan (Rome), with Kenneth Branagh and Jeany Spark

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The team is given cause for concern when an extremist Muslim cleric, encouraging violence in the West, is apparently assassinated by a Christian zealot. This leads the MI5 agents to believe it could be the start of a new wave of religious conflicts. Rupert Penry-Jones, Peter Firth and Hermione Norris star, with Shaun Dingwall and Lennie James

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Dan attempts heroics in a bid to rescue an 11-year-old from a canal boat where he is being held. The abductor turns out to be Rick Harris, whose addict son was found dead the previous day. June's private life gets entangled with public duties when she's called to boyfriend Rod's school to investigate one of his pupils - Kyle Henderson of the troublesome lottery-winning family - and there's a shock in store for her when the headmaster is critically injured in a hit-and-run incident

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is adjusting to life on her own after her father's wedding. She is engaged to Dr Marsham, but still unsure whether this is the right thing to do

programme.title: Birds of a Feather

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: In an effort to find out what life is like for Darryl behind bars, Tracey decides to build her own prison in the attic - with Sharon as her cellmate. Comedy, with Linda Robson and Pauline Quirke

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric agrees to help Michelle stay off drugs when she arrives at the hospital to inform him of social services' threats to take his grandson away from her. Donna joins Maria to look for a lost patient, and Linden is delighted when Callie finally wakes from her coma, but tries to protect her from the truth about the accident

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A day out with Baz seems destined to end in tragedy for Charlie when Louis is diagnosed with a rare fever. Luke persuades Bex to get checked over after she arrives late, looking pale and haggard - but the results of tests cause her to panic. Nikki recoils when Andy makes a pass at her, Comfort and Lara find themselves badly treated by men, and Roxy helps an injured robber

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on the home of larger-than-life lottery-winners the Hendersons and Stuart pursues a link with the hit-and-run incident that put Rod Jessop in hospital. Forensics reveal it is the third in a series of fires and the pressure is on to find the culprit before someone gets killed. There's good news for Sam, and June is so relieved to see Rod on the road to recovery she accepts his proposal

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark's debts are mounting. Kathy's still worried by Grant; if Phil has nothing to hide, why's he being so secretive? Tiffany and Sam have more in common than they realised

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark's bluff is called and he risks everything in a final rash bid. The police arrive for Grant, Pat gives David a lesson in parenthood, and Ian pushes his luck with Steve

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Anna is relieved when Gino believes she miscarried, and they spend the night together in the cottage before telling their families of their intention to marry. James sees a psychiatrist who agrees to treat his sexuality, Elizabeth worries that Regina is getting closer to discovering the truth about Sarah's past, and Jack is stunned to learn that he is Anna's father. However, a bigger shock is in store for those at Ash Park when a body is found in the river. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew and Kate's relationship sails into stormy waters as Richard's life hangs in the balance, and Alex has a fight on her hands to persuade a stubborn young man to undergo treatment for his breast cancer. Starring Gary Mavers, Maggie O'Neill, Lynsey Baxter and David Mallinson

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Helen practises her first-aid skills on James, and Siegfried is less than sympathetic when Tristan complains about deputising for Mrs Hall. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William plans a surprise party for Hester to celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary - despite her making it quite clear she expects nothing out of the ordinary to happen. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ballroom dancers Barry and Yvonne throw a party in their chalet, inviting only those members of staff they consider socially acceptable - which counts out comic Ted Bovis for a start. Jimmy Perry and David Croft's holiday camp comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth spots a strange man in Elizabeth's house and becomes convinced he has spent the night there

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Clegg, Billy and Truly hear an old school friend is about to die, the trio search for something - or someone - to put the spark back into his life. Comedy, guest starring Henry McGee, with Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Keith Clifford

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Following reports of Flambeau's death, the clerical sleuth races to protect a crown that contains a nail from the crucifixion of Christ. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: To find the killer of a beloved teacher at a swanky private school, Frankie enlists the help of a precocious student. Crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back in 1983, 16-year-old Amy Taskerland was murdered on the night of the school disco. More than 30 years later, the unearthing of a time capsule and the discovery of her voice on a mix tape inside prompts Miller and co to delve back into the case, starting by interviewing Amy's best friend Harriet and her former teacher Mr Hines. The recording features a strange mix of music and poetry, including words from a speech written in secret for the Queen for broadcast in the event of a nuclear war. But how did Amy get a copy of it, and was its secret nature the reason she was killed? Elsewhere, Miller enjoys her first romance since her divorce, while Gerry goes on his future son-in-law's stag night. Amy Nuttall (Emmerdale, Downton Abbey) and Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe) guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Adam is approached by his treacherous former partner Richard Dempsey, who sold him out during an operation in Serbia 15 years earlier. The MI5 agent asks for his one-time colleague's help when the team are assigned to protect a high-profile witness in the trial of a Serbian war criminal. Drama, guest starring Alec Newman, with Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on the home of larger-than-life lottery-winners the Hendersons and Stuart pursues a link with the hit-and-run incident that put Rod Jessop in hospital. Forensics reveal it is the third in a series of fires and the pressure is on to find the culprit before someone gets killed. There's good news for Sam, and June is so relieved to see Rod on the road to recovery she accepts his proposal

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: A brothel fire causes dark secrets to be revealed and the relationships between Eleanor, Joe and Major Quarrie to be finally resolved

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's heating breaks down, leaving them with no one to turn to - except their man-hungry neighbour Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe becomes head of the Public Duties Squad which supplies services to the private sector, leaving Gary and Margaret in charge of the restaurant. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Unwilling copper Crabbe and his team are assigned to guard the weary jurors of a long-running trial. Being holed up in the hotel from hell does nothing to improve the gourmet policeman's temper, and evidence of some serious jury-rigging only makes matters worse. Meanwhile, back at the restaurant, Sally is harassed by a finicky diner. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Obadiah Hakeswill returns to create more mischief, kidnapping a beautiful aristocrat and undermining the discipline of Wellesley's men. Sharpe is dispatched to thwart his old foe, and manages to inflict a humiliating defeat on French forces in the process - but only at terrible personal loss to himself. Sean Bean, Elizabeth Hurley and Pete Postlethwaite star

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A charity fundraiser who has travelled the country in a bath of baked beans is shot in a robbery, but his obsessive compulsive disorder gets on Gene's nerves, causing the DCI to beat a confession out of anyone who gets in his way. After the Clown warns Alex someone is going to die, she worries about Hunt's strong-arm tactics - and fights to keep control of the case. Matthew Macfadyen guest stars

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Alex realises there are just 24 hours until her parents die in a car bombing and chooses a horrified Ray to help her save them. Amid the chaos of a Gay Pride march, she arrests Tim and Caroline in the hope of keeping them safe, but with Lord Scarman due to visit the station a nervous Gene releases all the prisoners, aiming to make a good impression. Guest starring Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A young woman's body is found in a seemingly idyllic Northumberland coastal village in 1966, and the subsequent investigation leads Gently and Bacchus to suspect the victim's estranged husband is responsible for the killing. However, they soon come to realise her disturbed family is hiding an even more shocking secret. Starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Daniel Casey (Midsomer Murders), Shaun Dooley (Married Single Other), Paul Kaye (Two Thousand Acres of Sky) and Greg Hemphill (Still Game)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A bride-to-be and professional tennis player is found dead after her hen party, and the chief bridesmaid, best man and groom-to-be all come under the team's suspicion

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The new 'talkies' films are all the rage, meaning production of the exotic but silent Bride of Babylon is already in jeopardy - even before its lead actor is murdered on set. With one of Phryne's oldest friends producing the movie, she feels obliged to help identify the killer. Drama, starring Essie Davis

programme.title: Silent Witness

programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help Aliyah and Ajaz, who have two young children, making mealtimes hard work. Can Gregg and Chris help change their costly ways? Plus, Chris visits a chilli farm in Devon to try the world's hottest chilli and a team of tennis players taste test five lemonades

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate with a virtual audience from Lewisham asking questions of Culture Secretary Oliver Dowden, Shadow Foreign Secretary Lisa Nandy, mathematician and radio presenter Hannah Fry, chief executive of Next Simon Wolfson and actress-turned-activist Rose McGowan

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a semi in Surrey, a terrace in the West Midlands, and a bungalow in Ashton on Ribble, Lancashire

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Trading Standards Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. They also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims. Narrated by Harry Hepple

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles recounts the story of a base jumper who leapt from a cliff top, only fort his parachute to spin him 180 degrees into a rock face. Plus, the college students injured after their coach left the road and turned over

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from the Britannia Panopticon Theatre in Glasgow, looking back at some memorable 'musical buys' of the past and along the way dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers. Christina Trevanion also makes an appearance, paid a visit by Tim Weeks the gramophone guru. Plus, Anita Manning catches up with auctioneer Charles Hanson to learn more about singer and entertainer George Formby

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is joined by three inventors as customers test, review and score their precious products. In this episode, Mandy, Jonathan and Cait are all hoping to get the highest scores from the customers and be crowned the day's winner. Their initiatives are a smell-proof dog bag, a hand cream designed especially for nurses and a pirate-themed board game featuring 100 original characters

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah explores Arundel in West Sussex, in her quest for the ideal location as her buyers seek the right place to spend their £900,000 on. She hands over the keys to some surprising houses, but will her self-confessed 'instant buyers' decide to seal the deal on any of them?

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Designer-maker Sarah Peterson gets to grips with some old lampshades while blacksmith Kev Paxton is tasked with transforming some piling metal. Presenter Jay Blades bags himself some windbreaker poles, but will all three projects result in a profit for the items' unsuspecting owners? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Jane Cave, Tash Francis, Adi Higham, James Gooch and Ian Humphries compete to buy a classic 1970s Chopper bicycle, a bottle of Japanese whisky, a hand-written account of a First World War Zeppelin raid on Southend and a bed of nails. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the five remaining bands battle it out in tonight's semi-final

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley knows something is wrong with Mick and refuses to go anywhere until she knows the truth, forcing her son to delve into his childhood. Stuart and Rainie also have to face the music with their past transgressions, leaving lasting effects on their relationship. Can they move on and look to the future together?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former British number one tennis player Greg Rusedski, ex-England wicketkeeper Matt Prior, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and former Bolton Wanderers striker Kevin Davies join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Richard Ayoade hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian Roisin Conaty and writer and director Armando Iannucci to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: Over a gourmet meal of pork chop, chips, beans and tea, the subject under discussion yet again is Denise's impending wedding. Jim grumbles non-stop about having to foot the entire bill, because the groom's father has been on disability for 12 years. Denise, meanwhile, thinks Dave is not interested enough in the wedding

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before welcoming another group of Hollywood A-listers and British TV big shots to chat about what they've been up to recently. This week he's joined by pop queen Kylie Minogue, who chats and performs her current single Magic, British boxing champion-turned-Strictly contestant Nicola Adams, and actors Josh O'Connor and Emma Corrin, who are playing the roles of Charles and Diana in the new season of The Crown

programme.title: The Homesman (2014)
programme.desc: A single woman living in a frontier town is asked by her church to escort three women traumatised by the hardships of their lives to a safe refuge in the East. Along the way, she saves an outlaw's life and persuades him to help her protect her charges on the perilous journey through the wilderness. Western based on the novel by Glendon Swarthout, directed by and starring Tommy Lee Jones, with Hilary Swank, Meryl Streep and Miranda Otto

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v Liverpool and Everton v Manchester United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blues visit Lincolnshire Antiques & Home Show, assisted by Nick Hall and Gary Pe. Presenter Charlie Ross gives his top tips for bagging a bargain at an antiques fair

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a new home in South Yorkshire with room enough for their children and any future grandchildren. Plus, a dog-sledding race through the woods

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: In tonight's live final just four bands remain, and they'll perform for Little Mix and the viewers at home, who will be able to vote for the winner. After an exciting journey, which band will come out on top and win the prize of a lifetime supporting Little Mix on their next UK tour?

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 11 couples return to the Strictly Ballroom for movie week, performing routines inspired by, or paying tribute to, Hollywood films. Not only do the Strictly stars have to impress the judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but they also have to impress the viewers at home

programme.title: Festival of Remembrance
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents the Royal British Legion's annual Festival of Remembrance, a programme that has been pre-recorded due to the Covid-19 pandemic, paying tribute to the those who lost their lives as victims of conflict, from the First World War to the present day

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. Both league meetings between Everton and United ended 1-1 last term and another closely fought encounter was anticipated. The Blades came back from 2-0 down at Chelsea last term to rescue a point, but had struggled for form so far this term

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers

programme.title: Bobby (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a quirky cottage in Paddock Wood, Kent, a new build in Gnosall, Staffordshire, and a one-bedroom flat in Northampton

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey, Sarah Moore comes across items to salvage and sell on for a profit, including a retro bureau and two unloved chairs

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming: Compilation
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of High Court enforcement officers, who strive to retrieve money for others. Rob and Gerald brave the red carpet at a celebrity hotspot in London to try to recover the unpaid wages of a former worker. There is an angry confrontation in a London motorcycle shop when agents try to remove goods to collect a debt owed to a customer, and Michael and Jess help a man who was forced out of his job while recovering from cancer. Can they get him the large sum of money a tribunal awarded him?

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Author and former MP Edwina Currie relives a childhood holiday in Bournemouth from 1957, in the company of Len Goodman. Along the way, they talk about her life as a student, her Orthodox Jewish upbringing and the inevitable controversies brought about by her time in parliament

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George's article about the restaurant causes outrage in town and he worries about Hannah, who has been defending Terry since the beginning of their romance

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the five remaining contestants take part in the quarter-final, creating garments and trying their hand at techniques from around the world. Firstly, the sewers must follow a pattern like they have never encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a Filipino Terno Blouse. In the Transformation Challenge, the competitors are asked to turn Provencal tablecloths from France into a brand new outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a dramatic Flamenco skirt from Southern Spain in the final challenge

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The semi-final sees the hopefuls creating garments inspired by silver screen. First, they must follow a pattern like none they have encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a dress inspired by Marilyn Monroe's in The Seven Year Itch. In the Transformation Challenge, the sewers have to make a sci-fi inspired outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a made to measure flapper dress for the final task of the semi final

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts search for items at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales, where David Harper looks at vintage Second World War posters and Claire Rawle is surprised by a valuable watch

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mark Billingham, Neil Delamere, AJ Odudu and Lucy Porter test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal dishes more backstage gossip and raids the rails with costume designer Vicky Gill to find out what some of the couples will be wearing this weekend

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan goes behind the scenes of Drax Power Station, 14 miles from York in North Yorkshire, and the biggest power station in Britain. She follows engineers as they undertake a £50million maintenance programme that requires them to move equipment weighing 300 tons. There are also giant boilers and turbines to refurbish as they get one of their six generating units re-connected to the National Grid

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: The team focus on the incredible journeys made by wildlife in autumn. Megan McCubbin catches up with rare whales in the Scottish Highlands, Iolo Williams reveals the science behind nocturnal mass migration, Chris Packham and Megan light up the New Forest to visualise secret fungal networks, and Gillian Burke learns how ducks support their own team colours

programme.title: Being Frank: The Frank Gardner Story
programme.desc: The BBC security correspondent explores what it is like to suddenly become disabled, something he experienced himself 16 years ago after being shot by Al-Qaeda gunmen in Saudi Arabia. The bullets damaged his spinal nerves, leaving him partially paralysed and he has used a wheelchair ever since. In this deeply personal film, he talks candidly about the effects his injuries have had on his life, work, relationships and the way he views himself. And he speaks to others, to explore how they have responded to sudden life-changing injuries

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Ed Gamble, Sukh Ojla and Mark Simmons. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the events from May 2019

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events with Emily Maitlis and Emma Barnett

programme.title: Birds of Paradise: The Ultimate Quest
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thirty years ago explorer Benedict Allen lived in Papua New Guinea with the Niowra, a remote people. Broadcast journalist Frank Gardner has always wished to see wild birds of paradise, so Benedict resolves to take him along. The duo set out through some of the most inhospitable terrain on the planet, negotiating swamps, mountains and crocodile-infested waters, heading into the cloud forest. Despite Frank requiring the use of a wheelchair following a shooting incident in 2004, Benedict determines to get him to their destination

programme.title: Birds of Paradise: The Ultimate Quest
programme.desc: Part two of two. Explorer Benedict Allen is determined to get disabled journalist Frank Gardner into the wilds of Papua New Guinea, despite having to grapple with Frank's wheelchair. As the terrain gets even tougher to negotiate, the pair know they must make an epic journey into the highlands, crossing through two tribal territories to achieve their objective. However, as Frank gets close to realising his dream of seeing wild birds of paradise up-close, his old injuries return to haunt him and the expedition hangs on a knife edge

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin recalls the making of his third travel series Full Circle, a journey around the Pacific Rim which took him to Russia, China, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand and North and South America. This series saw Michael adopting a more investigative role than in previous odysseys, particularly when encountering the drug wars and poverty that plagued Colombia. With contributions from David Attenborough and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: The live-streaming of an attack on mosques in Christchurch, New Zealand, in March 2019 heralds a new era of terrorism and confirms the far right as a fast-growing threat. Survivor Abdul Aziz describes how he was praying when a fellow worshipper was shot through a window, and he ran outside to confront the attacker, while analyst Jacob Davey recalls monitoring the digital footprint of the attack

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: Return of the documentary that goes behind the scenes on the most complex murder and large-scale criminal investigations in Scotland. The detectives investigate the case of 37-year-old Paul Mathieson, who was assaulted and found fighting for his life on a street in Renfrew. The victim of a brutal assault, Paul succumbs to his horrific injuries, and the investigators of Police Scotland's Major Investigations Team take up the case

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young boy rushes for help after his grandfather falls down a cliff face, a road racing cyclist's career is threatened when he is hit by a car and a motorbike rider hits black ice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The progress of a three-bedroom semi in Crewe, Cheshire, a studio apartment in London's Hammersmith and a mid-terrace in Colne, Lancashire - all of which are bought as investments

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Stephen Kember is given a long-mute music box that once belonged to a professional stage magician and toy experts Julia Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch get their hands on a much-loved push-along horse that has seen better days. Other items up for repair are a water-damaged penny arcade game and an old leather-backed chair

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show in which a team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The Strictly judge takes TV presenter Valerie Singleton back to Ladram Bay in Devon to reminisce about her favourite childhood holiday she enjoyed there in 1952. For the one-time Blue Peter presenter the memories come flooding back as Len takes her back to the farm she once visited, cooks her the sort of fry-up she would have enjoyed at the time and whisks her around the dance floor at the campsite's clubhouse

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Zak, Ike and Steve are missing at sea and the town mounts a huge search-and-rescue operation. Antipodean comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Jan needs to know who the father of her twins is, and Woody has a dark secret about his wedding. Shay is relieved to have Ike back, but the boat accident has taken its toll on him

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of economic recipes, including a Bake Off masterclass featuring Sussex suet pond pudding, and a James Martin tomato soup with garlic bread

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contest, hosted by Joe Lycett, reaches its final stage as the last three contestants take on a trio of celebration wear challenges before the winner is announced. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young start by challenging the sewers to make a little boys kilt without a pattern. In the Transformation Challenge, they each attempt to turn party dresses into fun outfits fit to wear at a carnival, before finally creating unique gowns using luxury fabric in the Made to Measure challenge. At the end of the three challenges, one of the competitors is crowned this year's winner

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Nick Davies and David Harper are on hand in Reading, Berkshire to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Nick comes across a programme from the 1935 FA Cup match between local team Reading and Arsenal, while David discovers some Zulu artefacts from much further afield. Plus, presenter Paul Martin finds out how, in the 19th and 20th centuries, biscuit manufacturing put Reading's name on the map

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mark Billingham, Neil Delamere, AJ Odudu and Lucy Porter test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the week's ultimate winner finally declared

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal is joined by a trio of celebrity superfans to discuss all things ballroom. Gethin Jones is at Elstree Studios with the latest news from behind the scenes

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: Food writer Nigella Lawson shares the origins of some of her ideas, revealing just how and why they came into being, including a bowl of steamed clams with Thai basil inspired by a recent holiday in Thailand. Her toasty olive oil granola was born out of a need to have a satisfying breakfast at the ready, while hunger pangs at the other end of the day led to a dish of griddled halloumi with a sweet chilli sauce. Finally, Nigella's friends are treated to a supper of griddled bavette steak, followed by a lemon pavlova

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites Laura Whitmore, Anita Rani, Russell Kane and Alan Davies to discuss the books that take them back to their youth, the books that transport them to far-flung places and the books that make them feel positive about the world. They also talk about this week's book group picks - Small Pleasures by Clare Chambers and Alan Davies' new book Just Ignore Him

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Gillian reports on the return of giant blue-finned tuna to Cornwall. Plus, gannets take a leap of faith in Yorkshire, Iolo Williams joins leaping salmon in Wales, Michaela Strachan bids farewell to the seals and Chris Packham looks forward to Winterwatch in January

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The presenter explores some of the myths swirling around the French Revolution of 1789 - an uprising that brought down the nation's royal family. This violent revolution became the blueprint of many future revolutions across the world, but what exactly happened during this turbulent period is open to manipulation and interpretation. Historian Michael Rapport explains how the revolution was not started by starving peasants as many assume but was in fact sparked by a group of lawyers and property owners

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Melanie C found global fame and spread the girl power message as part of '90s girl group Spice Girls. Over the past 20 years, she has carved a successful solo career and her latest self-titled album - her eighth - released at the start of October, has been hailed by critics as her best so far. She talks to Jools about her life and career, illustrated by a diverse selection of clips from the extensive Later archive. This programme also features soul star and actor Mica Paris performing Take My Hand Oh Precious Lord and work from upcoming songwriter, producer and multi-instrumentalist David Balfe

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark, presents analysis of the day's national and international news stories, with Emily Maitlis in Washington DC

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Tigh's decision to impose martial law backfires as resource ships refuse to deliver supplies to Galactica. The Chief is arrested on suspicion of being a Cylon, and Gaius Baltar is enlisted to prove his fidelity. Lee helps President Roslin escape her imprisonment and an unlikely accomplice helps the pair flee to Cloud Nine, where they try to think of a way to restore order

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Adama resumes his post as commander of the Galactica, but is faced with the unenviable predicament of his crew losing faith in the cause. His first mission is to find his treacherous son and President Roslin, who seem to have formed a regrettable alliance

programme.title: Paths of Glory (1957)
programme.desc: A French assault on a German position during the First World War ends in disaster, but the general in charge refuses to accept the plan was doomed from the start and demands that three men from the ranks be executed as an example to the rest. However, the colonel who led the attack resolves to defend the condemned soldiers. Stanley Kubrick's drama, starring Kirk Douglas and Adolphe Menjou

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Chinese video-sharing service was the social media sensation of lockdown, an app that provided a bit of fun for people stuck indoors and now has hundreds of millions of users. In this report, Tina Daheley investigates whether it is safe for the millions of young people who have signed up to use it

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A team of transplant surgeons perform a double lung transplant operation, a procedure that must be carried out against the clock to avoid tissue deterioration. They successfully remove one diseased lung and replace it with a healthy one. However, just as they cut off the blood supply to the second lung, the transplanted organ stops working. At Addenbrooke's, a neurosurgeon removes a bundle of abnormal blood vessels sitting near the top of a patient's spinal cord

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Featuring a group of dogs riding Russia's underground rail system, and a clever crow that can use a pedestrian crossing to crack a tough problem

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his friends explore Cambodia's Angkor Wat Temple, the biggest religious monument in the world, and dance the macarena at a mythological theme park in Singapore

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team are out in the BP garden celebrating Bonfire Night this week. Lindsey Russell, Richie Driss, Mwaksy Mudenda and Adam Beales are cooking and toasting marshmallows over an open fire, they launch the BP Book Awards and there is a sneak peek behind the scenes of series two of BBC One hit drama His Dark Materials

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia reveals a harrowing past and Poppy takes over the show

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia and her friends hold the series wrap party in the flat

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to unearth the dinosaurs that were far deadlier than they first appeared. He discovers how the Sinornithosaurus hides a deadly dental secret, races a Velociraptor in a climbing challenge and puts Argentinasaurus to the ultimate test

programme.title: The Big Life Fix with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Designer and technologist Haiyan Zhang tries to come up with a monitoring solution for the parents of four-year-old William, whose rare form of epilepsy means they have to watch him around the clock, leaving them exhausted. Ian, 19, who has cerebral palsy, wants to find a way of taking control of a sailing boat, and 66-year-old multiple sclerosis sufferer Susan needs help operating the items in her home

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles in the arena, including a robot designed by a nine-year-old girl, which comes complete with a huge pink axe. Other entrants include a team of students from Falkirk whose robot is made of wood and has a petrol engine, and a family team with an ingenious creation made up of parts sourced from their local scrapyard

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features animals that utilise devious tactics to win a mate and raise a family, such as bower birds and fiddler crabs, who use optical illusions to impress a female, and male topi antelope, who fake danger to give them an edge. He also looks at family life, including the chicks that blackmail their parents, and the honeyguide bird that dupes another species into raising its young

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary histories of some of Britain's many islands, making a tempura dish from queen scallops caught off the Isle of Man and a rich cranachan with whisky from Islay. They also travel to the Isle of Wight to enjoy the cooking of a Michelin-starred chef and sample a Jersey shellfish delicacy

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Meerkat pups Squirt and Weeny belong to one of the most famous animal families on the planet and are also part of a 20-year animal behaviour study. David Attenborough narrates the full story of the research, from the early days when it was a challenge to even get close to a meerkat to the latest revelations about these animals and their complex lives

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman takes his dancing pal Anton du Beke back to Blackpool to reminisce about a seaside holiday in 1981, including taking him for a tide on a vintage tram

programme.title: On the Town (1949)
programme.desc: Musical following three sailors who are out for a good time while on a day's shore leave in New York. One tries initially to fend off the advances of a female taxi driver, another is pulled into helping with an anthropologist's study of prehistoric man, while the third seeks out the model in a famous advertising campaign - but finds she is not quite what he expected. With Gene Kelly, Frank Sinatra and Jules Munshin

programme.title: Ingenious Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts explore creatures' surprising physical characteristics, including how one man's understanding of an eagle's physical abilities could help save it from extinction. Conservationist Giles Clark witnesses an Australian kangaroo that is more at home climbing trees than hopping through the Outback, zoologist Lucy Cooke is in Costa Rica to find out if the sloth's laziness is the key to its success, and marine biologist Patrick Aryee reveals the secrets of one of the world's oddest looking creatures - the platypus

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: FC United of Manchester v Doncaster Rovers (Kick-off 5.30pm). Dan Walker presents all the action from the FA Cup first-round tie at Broadhurst Park. The non-league hosts reached this stage after defeating Guisleley in the final qualifying round and defeated Football League opposition at this stage of the 2010/11 competition when they won 3-2 away to Rochdale. However, they will still go into this match as underdogs against the League One outfit

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: In the final episode, Jamie Crawford takes to the skies, using aerial images to find out how the Scottish people have harnessed our precious natural resources to power our industry, from a remote loch near Ullapool to the Carron iron works of Falkirk. He also travels to Shetland to follow in the footsteps of his father, who went there to work during the oil industry boom

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Doctor McCeavedy accidentally discovers that Frazer keeps a hoard of gold sovereigns in his home and Mainwaring tries to persuade the undertaker that his life savings would be better kept in a safer place - namely his bank. Vintage comedy, with Arthur Lowe and John Laurie. First shown in 1977

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of reflections this week, with Joe Lycett, Zoe Lyons, Liza Tarbuck and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Perfect 10 (2019)
programme.desc: Fourteen-year-old Leigh lives on the outskirts of Brighton with her neglectful father. A talented gymnast, she is training hard for her first competition, despite a lack of confidence. But when an older half-brother she never knew existed turns up at the house one night, her lonely existence is altered, and turns her feelings on their head. Coming-of-age drama, starring Frankie Box, Alfie Deegan, Sharlene Whyte and William Ash

programme.title: Silence (2016)
programme.desc: In the 17th century, two Jesuit priests travel from Portugal to Edo-era Japan to locate their missing mentor and spread Catholic Christianity. Martin Scorsese's historical drama, starring Andrew Garfield, Adam Driver, Tadanobu Asano, Ciaran Hinds and Liam Neeson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The steeplejack visits Britain's arts, entertainment and leisure landmarks, including the Globe Theatre, Blackpool Tower and the Victoria and Albert Museum

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a frozen pond surrounded by snow drifts, warmed by a rich sky and late autumn foliage

programme.title: The Band Wagon (1953)
programme.desc: A has-been Hollywood dancer and a pretentious producer form an uneasy alliance to stage a song-and-dance spectacular. However, the latter's bizarre ideas soon spiral out of control, while the former loathes the ballerina cast opposite him. Musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire, Jack Buchanan and Cyd Charisse. Songs include That's Entertainment and Dancing in the Dark

programme.title: CripTales
programme.desc: Fictional monologues based on the experiences of disabled people. Jackie is in hospital bed, facing a decision about whether to have her leg amputated. As she thinks through her options, she explores her feelings for someone she has recently met, who seems interested in her. Written and performed by Jackie Hagan

programme.title: CripTales
programme.desc: A writer of children's literature finds herself falling in love with Keira, her next-door neighbour. But Ellie, her carer, does not approve of the relationship and starts to subtly block the romance - with dangerous consequences. Monologue, performed by Carly Houston

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part documentary exploring the shocking story of eugenics, the controversial idea that the human race can be improved by selective breeding. It is commonly thought that the idea disappeared after the horrors of the Nazi Holocaust, but as science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson discover, practices such as the sterilisation of the poor continued on a global scale for many more years

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: When Bonnie Prince Charlie arrives in Scotland to regain the crown of his ancestors, the clans are called upon to rise in armed revolt for him. Many do, and they invade England, but others oppose him and join the British side to fight against the rebels. Some though play a cunning double game, pretending to be loyal to the British whilst secretly raising clansmen for the Prince. Presented by Neil Oliver

programme.title: The Disordered Eye
programme.desc: Disabled artist and film-maker Richard Butchins questions whether good vision equals great art, and looks at modern and historical artists through the lens of their impaired vision

programme.title: Screengrabbed: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez presents a selection of short videos offering artists and film-makers the chance to tackle life's biggest topics. How do you know you're in love? What does it mean to be British? Illustrator and performer Jessie Cave and visual artist Sarah Maple are just two of those who give their refreshing take on these and other questions

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a frozen pond surrounded by snow drifts, warmed by a rich sky and late autumn foliage

programme.title: CripTales
programme.desc: Fictional monologues based on the experiences of disabled people. Jackie is in hospital bed, facing a decision about whether to have her leg amputated. As she thinks through her options, she explores her feelings for someone she has recently met, who seems interested in her. Written and performed by Jackie Hagan

programme.title: CripTales
programme.desc: A writer of children's literature finds herself falling in love with Keira, her next-door neighbour. But Ellie, her carer, does not approve of the relationship and starts to subtly block the romance - with dangerous consequences. Monologue, performed by Carly Houston

programme.title: The Sound of Movie Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and musician examines the period from the 1970s onwards, revealing how the musical would find its modern voice by adapting rather than trying to ape the classic formula of old. He meets Mel Brooks, creator of the unforgettable Springtime for Hitler, and Turner Prize-winning artist Jeremy Deller who argues that Ken Russell's film of The Who's rock opera Tommy is the best ever made. The composer and musician also looks at some of the other hit musicals of the era, such as Grease and Bugsy Malone

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on March 1, with performances by artists including Shakin' Stevens, Electribe 101, Michael Bolton, Rod Stewart, Jamie J Morgan, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton and JT & the Big Family

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on March 8, with performances by artists including Bros, Guru Josh, Marc Almond, Gloria Estefan, JT & the Big Family, Innocence, Erasure, David A Stewart featuring Candy Dulfer, Bros, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton and the B-52's

programme.title: Punk/New Wave Years with Annie Nightingale
programme.desc: The veteran radio and Old Grey Whistle Test presenter recalls a radical era in music, which dominated the pop landscape during the late 1970s. Back then, punk was hard to find on mainstream television and Annie sought to build a platform for young people to have their say. Here, Annie opens up the archive to select some of the finest and most intriguing moments from this era. Covering punk, post-punk and new wave, she explores a movement in music that became the soundtrack to a generation referencing clips featuring the Damned, the Sex Pistols, Iggy Pop, Patti Smith, Gang of Four, Linton Kwesi Johnson, Tubeway Army, Ian Dury and the Blockheads, Siouxsie and the Banshees, Soft Cell, the Selecter, Joy Division and the Au Pairs

programme.title: More Punk & New Wave Compiled by Annie Nightingale
programme.desc: BBC's first female DJ and face of The Old Great Whistle Test, Annie Nightingale, handpicks more of her favourite punk and new wave performances. This compilation features rare archive footage from the Slits, the Raincoats and Rhoda Dakar, as well as concert footage featuring Blondie and the Clash. Sprinkled throughout are live studio performances from Whistle Test, including a haunting performance of Ghosts by Japan, a classic Ramones clip and a rare John Foxx offering. The Teardrop Explodes and Tom Tom Club also feature, as do Adam Ant and Siouxsie Sioux

programme.title: Annie Nightingale: Bird on the Wireless
programme.desc: A profile of the DJ and broadcaster, who was hired as Radio 1's first female presenter in 1970, charting how her tastes have fluctuated during her career, which has seen her champion musical styles including punk, new wave, acid house and dubstep. Artists including Paul McCartney and the Clash's Mick Jones also discuss her impact on Britain's musical landscape

programme.title: Old Grey Whistle Test: Police in the East
programme.desc: Documentary following the Police on their 1980 tour of Japan, Hong Kong, India, Egypt and Greece. Annie Nightingale interviews band members Sting, Stewart Copeland and Andy Summers, and the programme also features footage from the concerts

programme.title: Punk/New Wave Years with Annie Nightingale
programme.desc: The veteran radio and Old Grey Whistle Test presenter recalls a radical era in music, which dominated the pop landscape during the late 1970s. Back then, punk was hard to find on mainstream television and Annie sought to build a platform for young people to have their say. Here, Annie opens up the archive to select some of the finest and most intriguing moments from this era. Covering punk, post-punk and new wave, she explores a movement in music that became the soundtrack to a generation referencing clips featuring the Damned, the Sex Pistols, Iggy Pop, Patti Smith, Gang of Four, Linton Kwesi Johnson, Tubeway Army, Ian Dury and the Blockheads, Siouxsie and the Banshees, Soft Cell, the Selecter, Joy Division and the Au Pairs

programme.title: The Sound of Movie Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and musician examines the period from the 1970s onwards, revealing how the musical would find its modern voice by adapting rather than trying to ape the classic formula of old. The composer and musician also looks at some of the other hit musicals of the era, such as Grease and Bugsy Malone

programme.title: Pipers of the Trenches
programme.desc: Around 2,500 pipers from Scotland, England, Ireland, Canada, Australia and New Zealand served in the First World War and within a year a thousand of them were dead. This film uncovers stories of bravery as descendants of those pipers visit the Somme and Gallipoli, the battlefields where their unarmed relatives accompanied their comrades over the top. Military historians provide insights into the courage of these men and the prospect they faced as they emerged from the trenches and led the troops into battle

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The actor and writer heads for Australia, where he helps a sick crocodile in the Northern Territory and encounters 'jackaroos' who round up camels in the desert around Alice Springs. After travelling to Sydney he plays a cameo role in soap Home and Away, before leaving for New Zealand, where he meets bungee jumpers and university freshmen in the Water of Leith

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Two men are brought in for questioning in Paris about the killing of Victoria and confess to having paid her to be the surrogate mother of their baby, while several leads take Rolf and Claire back to Poland. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Rolf, Claire and Karol do not get much help from the nuns as they try to find answers in a convent in Poland, while Rolf then discovers a connection to the case that points to a fertility clinic in Paris. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: Mimi & Co
programme.desc: In this episode, Mimi decides that it’s time for her little sister Erica to face her greatest fear – heights! We meet 12-year-old Sami, who with the help of his dad, has built a car that runs on drill power. Sami’s family have Syrian heritage, and they celebrate learning the best of both cultures. programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy leaps into his time-traveling clock to visit a fearsome sabre-toothed cat, but when his gizmo malfunctions he is accidentally shrunk down to the size of an insect

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk along the beach to go paddling in the sea.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's Jess's 'bring-a-pet' birthday party and Waffle competes with Pickles the parrot to be the star. Waffle has a surprise for everyone when he takes to the stage with Simon

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack tries to help Daisy by bringing Twinkle the pony to the vet's surgery. Twinkle escapes and ends up in the Big Hub - which doesn't please Moira at all

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Topsy dresses up on the day of Vinda's knights and princesses party, but Tim and Tony do not want to join in the fun

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Scarlet is cooking a colourful French vegetarian ratatouille

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Kobi is painting a wall but cannot work out which colour to use

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat: Rockpool Tales
programme.desc: Old Jack tells a story to his faithful companion Salty dog about the day a storm covers the rockpool with seaweed. Can Buster the Crab help with his big claws?

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm visits Yorkshire to find out all about pumpkins and joins some friends for a spooky pumpkin party

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: Russian Liza picks cucumbers with her grandmother, in India Anushka gets ready for school, while in Jordan Hamza helps at his father's barbershop

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers pretend to be trains on a walk to the library.

programme.title: Monty & Co
programme.desc: Monty and Eddie share a bedroom. Monty wants to write poetry while Eddie wants to drum. They divide the room in half with red tape.

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Lemon is annoyed the Leafs think her antlers are a tree, and won't leave her alone. The Olobobs make Treewheeler, who takes the Leafs on an exciting rocket ride in the sky

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble tries to be quiet as Grandad Tumble has a nap, while Justin and his friends do some quiet and loud dancing

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Benjamin gets a pet snail, but his friends cannot see the appeal, especially when it attracts the attention of Old Brown

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The heroes try to save the world's stinkiest plant being turned into a perfume

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran see Cynthia building a new greenhouse and decide to build a den. But when the den is too small both of them to fit in, Gran Gran helps JoJo with a plan they can follow to build a bigger den, one that will fit Gran Gran and Panda inside too!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory goes to meet Esme and Bingham the bearded dragon. They clean out the vivarium and provide fresh food, water and calcium to keep Bingham's bones strong

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this super fast episode, CBeebies’ very own motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that are powered by electricity! Grace rides a very unusual electric motorbike, checks out a solar plane and then drives an eco racer!

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with sponges and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: The puppies have a sleepover in Doug-Po's room, but keep falling out of bed

programme.title: CBeebies Prom: A Musical Journey
programme.desc: Jessica Cottis and the BBC Concert Orchestra perform a selection of inspirational classical pieces that take viewers around the world, back through time, and into space

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The Shallots want to explore beyond the front garden, so Sarah comes up with a plan

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lola decides to be helpful and good, but things soon go wrong when she accidentally spills pink milk on Charlie's homework

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee wakes up super-early and wants to play immediately. Mo wonders how much zooming is needed before everybody needs a nap

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Cloud has been on holiday in space, but when it returns the Clangers notice it is very cold. Mother Clanger is worried when she sees it snowing rather than raining, and she calls for Major Clanger to come and have a look. The Clangers have never seen snow before. Major Clanger finds it very interesting and decides to take a sample to his workshop to analyse, but he's amazed to see that it has disappeared and has been replaced by water. While Small Clanger and Baby Soup Dragon enjoy a snowball fight, Tiny Clanger spots The Cloud shivering and comes up with a clever plan. Will Tiny's plan be enough to stop The Cloud shivering?

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy dances in the garden and the Pinky Ponk copies her every move

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Eddie Redmayne reads Gabby Dawnay's If I Had a Dinosaur, a story about a little girl who wonders what kind of creature would make the best pet

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: A young boy rushes for help after his grandfather falls down a cliff face, a road racing cyclist's career is threatened when he is hit by a car and a motorbike rider hits black ice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Newmarket Racecourse, Suffolk, where two teams of best friends go head to head to maximise their returns, assisted by experts John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Anita also learns about a legend of horse racing who didn't set foot on the turf

programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown heads to Bideford in Devon to show his buyers a few properties for their £435,000, while also taking in community life, from its busy arts scene to its luxury ice-cream

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore gives an old tartan picnic rug to bag-maker Rosanna Gethin and a set of tired chairs to upcycler Rachel Henderson, while she restyles a vintage glass display cabinet

programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including pieces of pottery saved from a tip, two unusual fire screens and a mirror. Presented by Nigel Havers
