programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keegan accidentally knocks Rainie over on his bike, forcing her to admit she is pregnant to a horrified Tiffany and Keegan. Kat is annoyed that Phil turned down her offer, and while Kush suggests it's for the best, she refuses to let the idea drop. Linda arranges a medication review for Mick, who is furious that she has gone behind her back, and Jay has good news about a flat he and Lola were hoping to move in to - but she does not share his enthusiasm

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help Aliyah and Ajaz, who have two young children, making mealtimes hard work. To make matters worse, the couple have differing tastes, so they rarely eat the same meal. And when time is against them, the takeaway wins yet again. Can Gregg and Chris help change their costly ways? Plus, Chris visits a chilli farm in Devon to try the world's hottest chilli and a team of tennis players taste test five lemonades

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate with a virtual audience from Lewisham asking questions of Culture Secretary Oliver Dowden, Shadow Foreign Secretary Lisa Nandy, mathematician and radio presenter Hannah Fry, chief executive of Next Simon Wolfson and actress-turned-activist Rose McGowan

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles recounts the story of a base jumper who leapt from a cliff top, only fort his parachute to spin him 180 degrees into a rock face. Plus, the college students injured after their coach left the road and turned over

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from the Britannia Panopticon Theatre in Glasgow, looking back at some memorable 'musical buys' of the past and along the way dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers. Christina Trevanion also makes an appearance, paid a visit by Tim Weeks the gramophone guru. Plus, Anita Manning catches up with auctioneer Charles Hanson to learn more about singer and entertainer George Formby

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is joined by three inventors as customers test, review and score their precious products. In this episode, Mandy, Jonathan and Cait are all hoping to get the highest scores from the customers and be crowned the day's winner. Their initiatives are a smell-proof dog bag, a hand cream designed especially for nurses and a pirate-themed board game featuring 100 original characters

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah explores Arundel in West Sussex, in her quest for the ideal location as her buyers seek the right place to spend their £900,000 on. She hands over the keys to some surprising houses, but will her self-confessed 'instant buyers' decide to seal the deal on any of them?

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Designer-maker Sarah Peterson gets to grips with some old lampshades while blacksmith Kev Paxton is tasked with transforming some piling metal. Presenter Jay Blades bags himself some windbreaker poles, but will all three projects result in a profit for the items' unsuspecting owners? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Jane Cave, Tash Francis, Adi Higham, James Gooch and Ian Humphries compete to buy a classic 1970s Chopper bicycle, a bottle of Japanese whisky, a hand-written account of a First World War Zeppelin raid on Southend and a bed of nails. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the five remaining bands battle it out in tonight's semi-final

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley knows something is wrong with Mick and refuses to go anywhere until she knows the truth, forcing her son to delve into his childhood. Stuart and Rainie also have to face the music with their past transgressions, leaving lasting effects on their relationship. Can they move on and look to the future together?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former British number one tennis player Greg Rusedski, ex-England wicketkeeper Matt Prior, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and former Bolton Wanderers striker Kevin Davies join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Richard Ayoade hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian Roisin Conaty and writer and director Armando Iannucci to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Having previously done stints as farmer, butler, firefighter, primary school teacher, zookeeper, drag artist, police officer, wedding planner, scout leader, tour guide and male model, the stand-up comedian returns to try his hand at more jobs. He begins by training as a news reporter for BBC Wales Today, which involves him running round the streets in search of a headline story and learning what it's like to report from a war zone while trying to keep calm, remain impartial and land the scoop

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before welcoming another group of Hollywood A-listers and British TV big shots to chat about what they've been up to recently. This week he's joined by pop queen Kylie Minogue, who chats and performs her current single Magic, British boxing champion-turned-Strictly contestant Nicola Adams, and actors Josh O'Connor and Emma Corrin, who are playing the roles of Charles and Diana in the new season of The Crown

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: Documentary reinvestigating some of the country's most shocking unsolved murders, beginning with the case of 23-year-old Port Talbot woman Maureen Mulcahy, who was found strangled to death in February 1976. After a major breakthrough, the team discovers clues that suggest she may been the victim of a serial killer, and a new witness comes forward with new testimony

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v Liverpool and Everton v Manchester United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blues visit Lincolnshire Antiques & Home Show, assisted by Nick Hall and Gary Pe. Presenter Charlie Ross gives his top tips for bagging a bargain at an antiques fair

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple find a new home in South Yorkshire with room enough for their children and any future grandchildren, with a budget of £450,000. Away from the property search, Alistair meets a national sled dog champion and takes the reins on a high-octane race through the woods

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway appreciates the scenery of Berwyn Mountains in north-east Wales on a walk in which the presenter is accompanied by a pair of llamas. Derek also visits Tenby, leaving the tourist hotspots behind to discover more about the town's hidden past

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: In tonight's live final just four bands remain, and they'll perform for Little Mix and the viewers at home, who will be able to vote for the winner. After an exciting journey, which band will come out on top and win the prize of a lifetime supporting Little Mix on their next UK tour?

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 11 couples return to the Strictly Ballroom for movie week, performing routines inspired by, or paying tribute to, Hollywood films. Not only do the Strictly stars have to impress the judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but they also have to impress the viewers at home

programme.title: Festival of Remembrance
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents the Royal British Legion's annual Festival of Remembrance, a programme that has been pre-recorded due to the Covid-19 pandemic, paying tribute to the those who lost their lives as victims of conflict, from the First World War to the present day

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. Both league meetings between Everton and United ended 1-1 last term and another closely fought encounter was anticipated. The Blades came back from 2-0 down at Chelsea last term to rescue a point, but had struggled for form so far this term

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a round-up of the latest fixtures, including San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers

programme.title: Bobby (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans

programme.desc: Over a gourmet meal of pork chop, chips, beans and tea, the subject under discussion yet again is Denise's impending wedding. Jim grumbles non-stop about having to foot the entire bill, because the groom's father has been on disability for 12 years. Denise, meanwhile, thinks Dave is not interested enough in the wedding

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before welcoming another group of Hollywood A-listers and British TV big shots to chat about what they've been up to recently. This week he's joined by pop queen Kylie Minogue, who chats and performs her current single Magic, British boxing champion-turned-Strictly contestant Nicola Adams, and actors Josh O'Connor and Emma Corrin, who are playing the roles of Charles and Diana in the new season of The Crown

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Action from the latest Premiership fixtures, including Aberdeen v Hibernian at Pittodrie and St Johnstone v Kilmarnock at McDiarmid Park

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows. The team is joined by social media's Robert Borthwick, a man known for his wit and whimsy on Twitter that riles and rallies fans of every club in equal measure

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Cookery show featuring guest chefs and celebrities as well as highlights from the BBC Archives and wine choices for the dishes prepared in the studio

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook meets four generations of food lovers in Cardiff. While joining the Aslams to rustle up some of their favourite curries, fish dishes and a keema bolognese, Michela meets 90-year-old head of the family Mohammed, who came from Bangladesh in 1958 and went on to open one of Wales' very first Asian restaurants. Michela tries to prise the family's secret spice mix as well as helping grandson Yusuf to master one of his grandmother's specialities to bring along to a special party to celebrate his grandparents' 54th wedding anniversary

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v Liverpool and Everton v Manchester United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: In tonight's live final just four bands remain, and they'll perform for Little Mix and the viewers at home, who will be able to vote for the winner. programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing

programme.title: Festival of Remembrance
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents the Royal British Legion's annual Festival of Remembrance, a programme that has been pre-recorded due to the Covid-19 pandemic, paying tribute to the those who lost their lives as victims of conflict, from the First World War to the present day

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Chelsea v Sheffield United at Stamford Bridge. programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The progress of a three-bedroom semi in Crewe, Cheshire, a studio apartment in London's Hammersmith and a mid-terrace in Colne, Lancashire - all of which are bought as investments

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers investigate a travel company boss conning customers. They also examine a sewage leak which polluted a stream running through an Oxfordshire village

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young boy rushes for help after his grandfather falls down a cliff face, a road racing cyclist's career is threatened when he is hit by a car and a motorbike rider hits black ice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Newmarket Racecourse, Suffolk, where two teams of best friends go head to head to maximise their returns, assisted by experts John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Anita also learns about a legend of horse racing who didn't set foot on the turf

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara convinces Al to help her fix Harper, while Daniel and Bear try to find a new nurse for the Mill. Emma helps a mother and daughter get a new start

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown heads to Bideford in Devon to show his buyers a few properties for their £435,000, while also taking in community life, from its busy arts scene to its luxury ice-cream

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore gives an old tartan picnic rug to bag-maker Rosanna Gethin and a set of tired chairs to upcycler Rachel Henderson, while she restyles a vintage glass display cabinet

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including pieces of pottery saved from a tip, two unusual fire screens and a mirror. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Amol Rajan

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keegan accidentally knocks Rainie over on his bike, forcing her to admit she is pregnant to a horrified Tiffany and Keegan. Kat is annoyed that Phil turned down her offer, and while Kush suggests it's for the best, she refuses to let the idea drop. Linda arranges a medication review for Mick, who is furious that she has gone behind her back, and Jay has good news about a flat he and Lola were hoping to move in to - but she does not share his enthusiasm

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help Aliyah and Ajaz, who have two young children, making mealtimes hard work. To make matters worse, the couple have differing tastes, so they rarely eat the same meal. And when time is against them, the takeaway wins yet again. Can Gregg and Chris help change their costly ways? Plus, Chris visits a chilli farm in Devon to try the world's hottest chilli and a team of tennis players taste test five lemonades

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The trip down Apprentice memory lane concludes with a look back at what it takes for tasks to be a soaring success - and how to avoid an epic failure. There are jaw-dropping lows, including nauseating food failures and cringe-inducing pitches, as well as the best of the best - from TV selling superstars to those who clean up on dirty jobs. Alan Sugar, Karren Brady and Claude Littner reminisce about the best and worst moments as the candidates fought to avoid the taxi of shame

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: A review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate with a virtual audience from Lewisham asking questions of Culture Secretary Oliver Dowden, Shadow Foreign Secretary Lisa Nandy, mathematician and radio presenter Hannah Fry, chief executive of Next Simon Wolfson and actress-turned-activist Rose McGowan

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a semi in Surrey, a terrace in the West Midlands, and a bungalow in Ashton on Ribble, Lancashire

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Trading Standards Officers search the home of a loan shark and find a safe containing thousands of pounds. They also force a rogue builder to pay compensation to his elderly victims. Narrated by Harry Hepple

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles recounts the story of a base jumper who leapt from a cliff top, only fort his parachute to spin him 180 degrees into a rock face. Plus, the college students injured after their coach left the road and turned over

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from the Britannia Panopticon Theatre in Glasgow, looking back at some memorable 'musical buys' of the past and along the way dropping in on some of the show's favourite experts and auctioneers. Christina Trevanion also makes an appearance, paid a visit by Tim Weeks the gramophone guru. Plus, Anita Manning catches up with auctioneer Charles Hanson to learn more about singer and entertainer George Formby

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is joined by three inventors as customers test, review and score their precious products. In this episode, Mandy, Jonathan and Cait are all hoping to get the highest scores from the customers and be crowned the day's winner. Their initiatives are a smell-proof dog bag, a hand cream designed especially for nurses and a pirate-themed board game featuring 100 original characters

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah explores Arundel in West Sussex, in her quest for the ideal location as her buyers seek the right place to spend their £900,000 on. She hands over the keys to some surprising houses, but will her self-confessed 'instant buyers' decide to seal the deal on any of them?

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Designer-maker Sarah Peterson gets to grips with some old lampshades while blacksmith Kev Paxton is tasked with transforming some piling metal. Presenter Jay Blades bags himself some windbreaker poles, but will all three projects result in a profit for the items' unsuspecting owners? Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Jane Cave, Tash Francis, Adi Higham, James Gooch and Ian Humphries compete to buy a classic 1970s Chopper bicycle, a bottle of Japanese whisky, a hand-written account of a First World War Zeppelin raid on Southend and a bed of nails. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the five remaining bands battle it out in tonight's semi-final

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley knows something is wrong with Mick and refuses to go anywhere until she knows the truth, forcing her son to delve into his childhood. Stuart and Rainie also have to face the music with their past transgressions, leaving lasting effects on their relationship. Can they move on and look to the future together?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former British number one tennis player Greg Rusedski, ex-England wicketkeeper Matt Prior, Great Britain sprinter Bianca Williams and former Bolton Wanderers striker Kevin Davies join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Richard Ayoade hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian Roisin Conaty and writer and director Armando Iannucci to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: Over a gourmet meal of pork chop, chips, beans and tea, the subject under discussion yet again is Denise's impending wedding. Jim grumbles non-stop about having to foot the entire bill, because the groom's father has been on disability for 12 years. Denise, meanwhile, thinks Dave is not interested enough in the wedding

programme.desc: The comedian casts a millennial's eye over the rich pickings of the BBC archive. This instalment features a man who can communicate with chickens, Anneka Rice getting a cheeky proposition from the pokeman and people's reaction to the great soda farl shortage of 1976. Shane also brings his own brand of humour in original new sketches throughout.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair

Fantasy adventure, starring Jack Black and Dylan Minnette

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

The youngster accidentally releases them into the real world, and has to find a way to get them back in the books before they can cause chaos. Fantasy adventure, starring Jack Black and Dylan Minnette

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Goosebumps (2015)
programme.desc: A teenager discovers his new neighbour is the writer of a popular series of books, who harbours a terrifying secret. The ghosts and monsters the author writes about are real, and his stories keep them contained. The youngster accidentally releases them into the real world, and has to find a way to get them back in the books before they can cause chaos. Fantasy adventure, starring Jack Black and Dylan Minnette

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Professional American football player Rob Gronkowski moves into the neighbourhood, while Stewie uses steroids to increase his new bees' honey output, but it makes them aggressive

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois's allergy forces Brian to move into Stewie's bedroom, but the two have a difficult time as roommates, while Peter branches out and takes up DJing

programme.title: Celebrity Juice
programme.desc: Keith Lemon hosts the near-the-knuckle comedy quiz, with Jimmy Carr, Marvin Humes, Maya Jama and the Vamps' Bradley Simpson and Connor Ball joining Laura Whitmore and Emily Atack

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After the local airline pilots go on strike, Quagmire looks for new work and becomes a gigolo. When his clients begin to avoid paying for his services, he asks Peter for help getting his money. Meanwhile, Brian is kicked off Peter's health insurance, so he gets a job at a hardware store, until Stewie gets him fired and things 'get medical'

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: James Woods takes viewers behind the scenes at the Family Guy studios to offer a peek at a production week of the series to see what goes into the making of the show

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: With a two-on-two basketball tournament approaching, Roger's alter-ego, local basketball legend 'Billy Jesusworth' breaks his ankle and is unable to compete at his usual level. Stan reluctantly invites him to play with his league of 'old heads', but his act of kindness backfires when Roger steals away Stan's tournament partner

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A mishap at home with charity drive chocolates lands Steve in trouble at school, and he is placed in the 'At-Risk' class with the student body's worst trouble-makers

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland Jr is forbidden to wear his giant pancake outfit on Halloween because his father deems it too childish, so tries to reinvent himself as a cool teenager

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland and friends visit Goochland for the annual football match against their high school rivals, but an accident leaves them scrambling to find an escape from their enemies' territory. Meanwhile, Donna's provocative Halloween costume gets her into trouble with the police, and Roberta and Cleveland Jr play tricks dressed as their parents

programme.title: The Emily Atack Show
programme.desc: The actress and television personality hosts a show about the trials and tribulations of modern life, featuring sketches, stand-up and impressions. In the first episode, Emily tackles the theme of dating, and opens up about her own experiences, from being single to one-night stands, and also shares her top tips for flirting

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: YouTube stars Alfie Deyes, Joe Sugg and Marcus Butler enter a forest at dusk and endure a series of spooky challenges in order to win cash prizes. Reggie Yates hosts

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy is alarmed when Liz tells him Fraser is out of prison. Ken wants to give his relationship with Deirdre one last try. Sally warns Ashley that Mike is selling dodgy goods

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia encourages the Duckworths to leave her in charge of the pub. Deirdre tells Ken they should remain just as friends. Becky buys Ashley a Valentine's card

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is furious with Sophie for leaving him in the lurch. The Dingles are curious to find out what Zak's new job is. Roy finds Biff and Kelly in a passionate clinch

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is furious when Vic tells her about Paddy and Viv. Jan asks Zoe if she can take over Linda's job. Robert refuses to visit Jack on his 50th birthday

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local farmer Colin Judd is devastated when he is forced to place his property in the hands of an auctioneer following the death of his father - but is even more shocked to see his estranged brother at the sale. Debbie Bellamy finds herself in trouble for drink-driving, and David has difficulty with a stubborn pony meant to be pulling a trap full of tourists. Rural drama, with David Lonsdale

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bernie becomes the target of a series of mysterious and violent attacks, while Peggy is forced to intervene after David finds he can't match the other contestants in an angling competition. Helen is horrified by the arrival of her estranged father in Aidensfield - but he has a bombshell to drop that causes her to rethink their relationship. Rural drama, starring Peter Benson and Sophie Ward

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack goes to see Sarah, but she refuses to listen to him. Butch tries to impress Tara at work, and disaster strikes the orienteering course

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tony frantically searches for Will, Lyn and Dean in the dark. Lisa strongarms Zak into telling her what he has been doing each day. Kelly and Biff spend the night together

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Anne causes Curly to miss a meeting with Eric, which leads to Watts being sacked. Liz is stunned to discover Fraser is buying The Hourglass, and Tricia goes into labour

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia is not allowed to hold her baby as he is premature. Curly tells Anne he wants nothing more to do with her. Alan advises Liz not to antagonise Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate when the body of a woman is pulled from the river during the local Oak Apple carnival. Closer inspection suggests the drowning was deliberate - and, as the detectives find out more about the villagers, they learn the killing could be linked to the death of the victim's daughter eight years previously. John Nettles and Jason Hughes play the intrepid policemen, with guest star Simon Callow

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: The funerals approach, and without the answers he needs and still more questions being thrown up from White House Farm, Stan's efforts remain fruitless. When all looks lost for Stan and Mick solving the case, a vital witness comes forward. Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into the tragic events. Starring Alfie Allen, Mark Addy and Andrew Frame

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Debbie Bellamy goes on a late-night drinking spree by the river, but her actions get her into trouble when evidence ties her to the spot where a body is fished out of the water. Rob and Helen try to patch up their relationship, while Peggy and David are appalled to find a hippie encampment in the back yard - until they realise there's money to be made. Rural drama, with Josefina Gabrielle

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan is shocked to find Kelly in bed with Biff. Sarah refuses to let Jack collect any of his possessions. Kathy challenges Doug about the woman in the photo

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric sends Sam and Marlon to France to pick up cheap booze. Betty tells Biff to take some time away to help him get over Linda. John reveals his relationship with Steve to Tara

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona worries that Steve could get hurt by Fraser's friends. Liz is grateful when Fraser gives her a pay rise. Tricia is thrilled when she is allowed to hold her baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha confides in Curly that she hates sex. Ashley instructs Don to keep out of his love life. Liz is stunned when Fraser buys her a car

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin prepares to face his hearing, but despite having closed the practice, he finds all his patients are trying to make appointments. An American tourist who visited Portwenn previously returns to the town, hoping to research her family tree. However, she also has a medical issue, and comes to the troubled doctor seeking his advice. Louisa goes to see Chris to find out how serious the situation is, while the townsfolk take steps to show Martin how much he is appreciated. Sigourney Weaver reprises her guest role from the previous series

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Forty-year-old mother of three Samantha Whyte turns to the programme for help as she searches for the Swiss father she has never met, having spent years dreaming about it. Raised by her single mother in Glasgow, Scotland, she was told the story of her parents' whirlwind romance in Zurich, and became desperate to know the man behind the myth. Meanwhile, Vanda James looks to put an end to a secret that has haunted her family for more than 50 years. When she was a child, a new-born baby was taken away at a few days old, and her mother never spoke about it. She discovered the baby was her brother and had been adopted, but her mother died before she had the courage to ask her about him

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: With the testimony of a vital witness confirming some of Stan and Mick's suspicions, Taff still remains unconvinced, but the police are now forced to question what they thought they knew previously, and pursue the case against Jeremy. Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into these tragic events. Starring Mark Addy, Alexa Davies, Freddie Fox and Tom Christian

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: The evidence is examined as Jeremy's trial begins, with dramatic and conflicting witness testimony being given before the jury and the packed public gallery, and the jury give their verdict on what really happened that fateful night at White House Farm. Starring Freddie Fox, Alfie Allen and Stephen Graham

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Emma confesses to Alina that she accepted a share of the bistro robbery money and gave it to Steve to help Oliver. Abi is suspicious when Debbie makes out she is a high-flyer and is due in town for a work meeting. Michael is shocked to see Grace at the hospital, flanked by two prison officers

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Carry On Again Doctor (1969)
programme.desc: A disgraced surgeon sets up a clinic in the hope of promoting a supposedly miraculous slimming potion made from secret ingredients discovered on a remote tropical island. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jim Dale, Barbara Windsor, Charles Hawtrey, Hattie Jacques, Pat Coombs and Joan Sims

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. However, their investigation is made all the more difficult by the complex relationships of the victim's former fellow residents. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia take the lead roles in the second of two mystery dramas, with an all-star supporting cast including Caroline Catz, Hugh Dennis, Michele Dotrice, Freddie Fox, Suzanne Packer and Joanna Scanlan

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: In Hampshire, Becky spends her days as governess to Sir Pitt Crawley's neglected daughters, but her ambition is to get into her employer's good books and soon she is promoted to the position of his secretary. When Sir Pitt's sister Matilda pays a visit to Queen's Crawley, Becky forms a new plan and quickly charms her way to becoming her favourite new companion. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel, starring Olivia Cooke

On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Debbie Bellamy goes on a late-night drinking spree by the river, but her actions get her into trouble when evidence ties her to the spot where a body is fished out of the water. Rob and Helen try to patch up their relationship, while Peggy and David are appalled to find a hippie encampment in the back yard - until they realise there's money to be made. Rural drama, with Josefina Gabrielle

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jan is shocked to find Kelly in bed with Biff. Sarah refuses to let Jack collect any of his possessions. Kathy challenges Doug about the woman in the photo

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Eric sends Sam and Marlon to France to pick up cheap booze. Betty tells Biff to take some time away to help him get over Linda. John reveals his relationship with Steve to Tara

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona worries that Steve could get hurt by Fraser's friends. Liz is grateful when Fraser gives her a pay rise. Tricia is thrilled when she is allowed to hold her baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha confides in Curly that she hates sex. Ashley instructs Don to keep out of his love life. Liz is stunned when Fraser buys her a car

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin prepares to face his hearing, but despite having closed the practice, he finds all his patients are trying to make appointments. An American tourist who visited Portwenn previously returns to the town, hoping to research her family tree. However, she also has a medical issue, and comes to the troubled doctor seeking his advice. Louisa goes to see Chris to find out how serious the situation is, while the townsfolk take steps to show Martin how much he is appreciated. Sigourney Weaver reprises her guest role from the previous series

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Forty-year-old mother of three Samantha Whyte turns to the programme for help as she searches for the Swiss father she has never met, having spent years dreaming about it. Raised by her single mother in Glasgow, Scotland, she was told the story of her parents' whirlwind romance in Zurich, and became desperate to know the man behind the myth. Meanwhile, Vanda James looks to put an end to a secret that has haunted her family for more than 50 years. When she was a child, a new-born baby was taken away at a few days old, and her mother never spoke about it. She discovered the baby was her brother and had been adopted, but her mother died before she had the courage to ask her about him

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: With the testimony of a vital witness confirming some of Stan and Mick's suspicions, Taff still remains unconvinced, but the police are now forced to question what they thought they knew previously, and pursue the case against Jeremy. Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into these tragic events. Starring Mark Addy, Alexa Davies, Freddie Fox and Tom Christian

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: The evidence is examined as Jeremy's trial begins, with dramatic and conflicting witness testimony being given before the jury and the packed public gallery, and the jury give their verdict on what really happened that fateful night at White House Farm. Starring Freddie Fox, Alfie Allen and Stephen Graham

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Emma confesses to Alina that she accepted a share of the bistro robbery money and gave it to Steve to help Oliver. Abi is suspicious when Debbie makes out she is a high-flyer and is due in town for a work meeting. Michael is shocked to see Grace at the hospital, flanked by two prison officers

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Carry On Again Doctor (1969)
programme.desc: A disgraced surgeon sets up a clinic in the hope of promoting a supposedly miraculous slimming potion made from secret ingredients discovered on a remote tropical island. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jim Dale, Barbara Windsor, Charles Hawtrey, Hattie Jacques, Pat Coombs and Joan Sims

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. However, their investigation is made all the more difficult by the complex relationships of the victim's former fellow residents. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia take the lead roles in the second of two mystery dramas, with an all-star supporting cast including Caroline Catz, Hugh Dennis, Michele Dotrice, Freddie Fox, Suzanne Packer and Joanna Scanlan

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: In Hampshire, Becky spends her days as governess to Sir Pitt Crawley's neglected daughters, but her ambition is to get into her employer's good books and soon she is promoted to the position of his secretary. When Sir Pitt's sister Matilda pays a visit to Queen's Crawley, Becky forms a new plan and quickly charms her way to becoming her favourite new companion. Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel, starring Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A misunderstanding between Sid and Betty leads to chaos and confusion. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Richard Henwood delves into the ITV Sport archives to take a look back at James Hunt, Barry Sheene and a spectacular crash by Kelvin Burt

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Mysterious events unfold involving a beautiful blonde, and Templar gets behind the wheel of a rally car. Last-ever episode of the vintage adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The Sheriff of Nottingham tries to avoid paying his debts to a Jewish moneylender by inciting a riot. Fantasy drama, starring Michael Praed

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed's friends, Bill and Laura Basset, find themselves in trouble when some unwelcome visitors turn up at their house. Luckily, Steed is also planning a visit. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is captivated by an attractive female vocalist, who boasts limited musical talent. Nevertheless, he uses his contacts to secure a gig for her at a club run by a dodgy entertainment manager, and soon both he and Terry find themselves immersed in the seedier side of the showbiz industry during a night's work in a disco. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A surprising connection emerges when Haskins is framed for corruption, while Regan and Carter investigate a spate of break-ins. Police drama series, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Snooker: Champion of Champions Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day four from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring two best-of-seven-frame group semi-finals. Presented by Jill Douglas with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Neal Foulds and Alan McManus, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to prompt Arab cultural attache and assassin Rahad into carrying out a murderous deed, in the hope they can catch him red-handed just before he commits the act and force him out of the country. Comedy actor John Junkin guests stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.desc: Boxer Mike Tyson and darts player Eric Bristow discussing their crafts, there os a profile of Red Rum, and Martin O'Neill and Roy Keane talk about their managerial relationship

programme.title: Snooker: Champion of Champions Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day four from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the concluding group final, played over the best of 11 frames. Ronnie O'Sullivan was the final player to progress from this stage last year, defeating his long-time rival John Higgins 6-3 as he continued his bid to retain the title. Presented by Jill Douglas with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Neal Foulds and Alan McManus, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage 15, with riders tackling a 178km course from Mos to Puebla de Sanabria. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.desc: Documentary featuring dash-cam video footage of a host of astonishing accidents across South America and China, showcasing some of the wildest footage involving trucks

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: The slaying of a Texas ranger leads Murtaugh and Riggs to believe a possible misuse of power is at play in the LA Sheriff's department. Just as the duo are certain they have found their culprit, the case becomes even more complicated when a dark secret is dug up regarding Captain Avery's past. It turns out he may once have done the wrong thing for the right reasons, but can he convince those around him his cause was just? Damon Wayans and Clayne Crawford star in this series, based on the hit, big-screen movie

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is asked for protection by newsagent Mr Mukerjee, who has been sent death threats and had a brick thrown through his window. programme.title: The Professionals

Comedy actor John Junkin guests stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A villain comes out of hiding in an attempt to retrieve stolen loot and only Regan and Carter can stop him. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: An engineer hatches a plan to immortalise people by freezing them and bringing them back to life, and decides Simon Templar would make the ideal guinea pig

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: Robin is bewitched by evil sorceress Lilith, who sends him on a mission to steal the magical silver arrow from Herne's cave. Michael Praed stars

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Two Russian spies escape from the prison Oldhill Monastery, with the help of some lizard vodka, and eliminate their targets. The third is about to escape - and his plan is to assassinate Steed. Patrick Macnee stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Terry rescue Greek-Cypriot Charlie from a beating and learn that his cousin is being threatened by another relative who wants to take over their restaurant. Encouraged by Charlie who is vowing revenge, Terry takes a job in the establishment to try to catch the tormentors red-handed

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect a deposed African politician, who is due to attend a conference with a view to regaining power. After foiling two assassination attempts, they soon appreciate others share information on his whereabouts. Charles Dance and Geoffrey Palmer feature alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a profile of England football manager Gareth Southgate, the story of South Africa captain Siya Kolisi and an interview with former boxer Chris Eubank

programme.title: Snooker: Champion of Champions Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening semi-final on day five from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Ronnie O'Sullivan's reign as champion came to an end at this stage last year when he was beaten 6-5 by Neil Robertson, who went on to take The Rocket's crown in the final. Presented by Jill Douglas with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Neal Foulds and Alan McManus, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage 16, with riders tackling a 177.7km course from Salamanca to Ciudad Rodrigo. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A suspect collapses while being questioned and rushed to hospital with injuries likely to have been incurred in an assault. Though innocent, Regan finds himself targeted by the man's vengeful brother who thinks the detective is responsible for his sibling's condition. Gwen Taylor guest stars in the police drama, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team are at Wincanton, where they look ahead to this afternoon's racing. Plus, news from the final day of the flat season at Doncaster

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore presents action from December 1974, including Millwall v Portsmouth, Sheffield United v Arsenal and Stoke City v West Ham United

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Arsenal v Manchester United. Gabriel Clarke introduces coverage of the 1979 FA Cup final at the old Wembley Stadium, which featured the likes of Pat Jennings and Liam Brady for the Gunners, and Joe Jordan and Jimmy Greenhoff for United. With commentary from Brian Moore and Brian Clough

programme.title: ITV Sport Stories
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents highlights from the ITV Sport archive, including the inside story of the 1993 Grand National that never was. Plus, how New Zealand's rugby rallying cry the Haka was reinvented, and sprinter Linford Christie opens up about his failed drugs test at the 1988 Olympic Games

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 2.25 Rising Stars Novices' Chase, followed by races at 3.00 and 3.35 at Wincanton. Plus, racing from Doncaster, including the 3.15 November Handicap, and action from Aintree. With analysis from Luke Harvey and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Oli Bell, Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Lonely Are the Brave (1962)
programme.desc: A rebellious modern-day cowboy breaks out of jail and escapes on horseback - but reckons without the relentlessness of the authorities, who give chase by car and helicopter. A dogged sheriff leads the pursuit, coming to sympathise with his quarry. Western, starring Kirk Douglas, Walter Matthau, Gena Rowlands and George Kennedy

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Lonely Are the Brave (1962)
programme.desc: A rebellious modern-day cowboy breaks out of jail and escapes on horseback - but reckons without the relentlessness of the authorities, who give chase by car and helicopter. A dogged sheriff leads the pursuit, coming to sympathise with his quarry. Western, starring Kirk Douglas, Walter Matthau, Gena Rowlands and George Kennedy

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits southern Africa to see if he can track down a deadly fish that lurks in the eerie waters of the Okavango. A killer with shark-like teeth, there are stories of it hunting in packs and feeding on schooling fish or even human bodies. Jeremy is determined to find out if it is a giant piranha or something equally terrifying

programme.title: Snooker: Champion of Champions Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the second semi-final on day six from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames, as the line-up for the showpiece match is confirmed. Mark Allen's break of 140 at this stage last year was the highest of the 2019 tournament, but he went on to lose the match 6-4 to 2019 world champion Judd Trump. Presented by Jill Douglas with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Neal Foulds and Alan McManus, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Cycling: La Vuelta a Espana Highlights 2020
programme.desc: Action from stage 17, with riders tackling a 175.8km course from Sequeros to Alto de la Covatilla. Gary Imlach presents, with Daniel Friebe reporting and Ned Boulting and David Millar on commentary

programme.title: Gone in 60 Seconds (2000)
programme.desc: A veteran car thief is forced out of retirement to steal 50 top-of-the-range vehicles in exchange for his brother's life. While his sibling is held hostage by a sadistic gangster, he asks his former cronies to help - but with the clock ticking and just a few days remaining to pull off the daunting task, their chances of success are hanging by a thread. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Angelina Jolie, Giovanni Ribisi and Robert Duvall

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is asked for protection by newsagent Mr Mukerjee, who has been sent death threats and had a brick thrown through his window. programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to prompt Arab cultural attache and assassin Rahad into carrying out a murderous deed, in the hope they can catch him red-handed just before he commits the act and force him out of the country. Comedy actor John Junkin guests stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.title: The Saint

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood

programme.title: The Avengers

Presented by Jill Douglas with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Neal Foulds and Alan McManus, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Back in 2017, George drafted in architect Chris Mason to help Wendy and Allan, whose Colchester home resembled a block of flats. They ended up with an enormous double-height extension and a Roman-style portico, but the ambitious scheme blew their budget and the project remained unfinished. George returns to find out whether they have managed to finish the property to the same exceptional standards

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Alex Horne sets another selection of inventively pointless tasks for the celebrity quintet of Daisy May Cooper, Johnny Vegas, Katherine Parkinson, Mawaan Rizwan and Richard Herring. Daisy flirts with a security guard, Richard is surprised by some chickens and Johnny convinces Alex to wear sparkly shoes while operating heavy machinery. As ever, Greg Davies decides who is the best of the worst

programme.title: Maxxx
programme.desc: Everyone is talking about Maxxx again when his antics go viral on social media. He and his friends get an invitation to an exclusive showbusiness party, where he tries to strike a record deal. Comedy, written by and starring O-T Fagbenle

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including This Morning, The Million Pound Cube, Strictly Come Dancing, The Undoing, The Yorkshire Vet, First Dates, BBC news reports on the US election and a BBC Breakfast report on the Welsh shopping ban

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back contributors whose differences were the making, or breaking, of their dates, including pharmacist Peter and German shoe manufacturer Gregor. Elsewhere, 26-year-old Nathan may have made a name for himself on the Watford club scene, but could he get on the right track with businesswoman Jade?

programme.title: Blackout
programme.desc: Feature-length docu-drama exploring the effects of a devastating cyber attack on Britain's national grid. Based on expert advice and research, the film combines actual footage recorded during power cuts and other emergencies with fictional scenes and news stories to build a realistic account of the nation being plunged into darkness. Eyewitness reports reveal the impact of the crisis on hospitals, law and order, transport and food and water supplies, as the programme reveals how society might descend into chaos and anarchy during a prolonged blackout

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to New Orleans to visit Zeke's, a much-loved restaurant that has seen standards slip since the original owner died. The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Investigates
programme.desc: Kate Quilton investigates the 2019 listeria outbreak in UK hospitals that left six dead and asks if enough is being done to protect vulnerable patients from a failing food safety system. She meets scientists to uncover just what listeria is and how the outbreak happened, goes inside a hospital kitchen to see first-hand how ood is made and speaks with an anonymous government whistleblower who reveals cutbacks could mean another deadly outbreak will happen again

programme.title: Nazi Treasure Hunters
programme.desc: The treasure-hunting team travels to Germany, Holland, the USA, Spain and Switzerland to investigate Hermann Goering's massive multi-country art-looting operation, as they identify and track treasures that are still missing today

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: As the furlough scheme comes to an end, the programme follows the response to a job advert for a minimum-wage server role at a Manchester restaurant. Almost 1,000 people apply, including Katie, a cabin services attendant; Faye, a cruise ship dancer; Jake, a hotel manager; and John, a ticket inspector. We get to know them, as well as Abi, the recruitment consultant in charge of narrowing down the field; and Kerry, a mum-of-two who lost her job at Bentley and is desperate to find a new position to avoid the family losing their home. Through the lens of this one position, Dispatches explores the jobs crisis brought on by Covid that is threatening to push unemployment over three million by Christmas

Comedy, guest starring Christine Baranski
