Taking the Regency period as his inspiration, the chef conjures up a fairytale feast owing debts of gratitude to a selection of weird and wonderful childhood fantasies. He prepares a Cinderella-style pumpkin for starters, and a stuffed boar's head taken from Snow White, before treating his guests to an edible Hansel & Gretel house

programme.title: Surviving the Stone Age
programme.desc: Eight experts recreate life in Stone Age Europe, living as a tribe and using the resources and primitive tools of the time to hunt for food and build shelter. In the first episode, the team makes their home in an ancient river valley, with only basic rations, animal hides and a canoe to support them. Two of the tribe sets off on a hunting expedition, but as supplies dwindle, they begin to realise it is time to move on to pastures new

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns

She discovers how moving up the social ladder in medieval times required a whole new wardrobe of clothing and accessories and gets top grips with a historically accurate crossbow. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson uses the latest drone technology to look at the geographical features that shaped the city's development

Under the supervision of Dr Amir Khan, 12 overweight food lovers go on some of the most extreme diets out there. They include meat-lover Emma, whose love of sausage sandwiches is not helping, and Lynne, whose large takeaways covered in salt have pushed her into the obesity category. They and the others agree to try to shift 14 pounds in a single month

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors

programme.title: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Helen has an aneurysm in her brain that could burst at any time and lead to her death within minutes. For doctor Sanjeev Nayak there are no second chances as he embarks on an extraordinary operation to save his patient. Michael's life is also on a knife edge. After collapsing with a cardiac arrest, he was saved by a neighbour giving him CPR. But now the trauma doctors face a double battle - they must quickly assess his head injury and also remove a clot in his artery that is destroying his heart

Beginning her trip in Staffa, Susan visits the cathedral-like cavern of Fingal's Cave, one of the UK's true natural wonders formed over thousands of years. Her need for speed is fulfilled when she witnesses the Mull Rally, while in Tobermory, she visits a biscuit factory run on sustainable energy. Lastly, the presenter goes in search of the largest bird of prey in the UK - the white-tailed sea eagle

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath

programme.desc: A woman is found murdered and Gibbs and McGee are called in when she is discovered to be carrying ID bearing the name Sgt Patricia Moreno, a US marine known to have died several years earlier. The victim is discovered to have come to America from Afghanistan and a necklace reveals a fingerprint that leads the team to a known assassin. Guest starring Lolita Davidovich

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team works with Abigail Borin of the Coast Guard Investigative Service after a highly suspicious explosion and fire on board an oil rig kills a US marine corps reservist. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal

programme.title: Rawhide (1951)
programme.desc: Outlaws hold staff and a passenger and her baby hostage at a remote stagecoach station while awaiting the arrival of a shipment of $100,000 in gold bullion. The nervous captives try to stay calm, but the tension between the gang leader and a jumpy henchman grows fraught, ending in a frantic shoot-out - with the infant in the thick of the fray. Western, starring Tyrone Power, Susan Hayward and Jack Elam

programme.title: Millions Like Us (1943)
programme.desc: A woman is happy to be called up for service during the Second World War, but disappointed to learn she will be working in a factory making aircraft parts. However, the new job brings romance in the shape of a handsome airman and a close circle of friends in her co-workers, who all rally round when tragedy strikes. Drama, starring Patricia Roc, Eric Portman, Gordon Jackson and Basil Radford

programme.title: The Savage (1952)
programme.desc: A white man raised by the Sioux after his family were killed by Crow warriors finds his loyalties tested in later life. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Susan Morrow and Peter Hansen

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly (1966)
programme.desc: A cynical, mercenary cattle drover sets out to deliver a herd to soldiers at a Union base during the American Civil War. During the journey, he is kidnapped by a Confederate colonel, who hopes to persuade him to help steal the animals and take them deep into rebel territory. Western, starring William Holden, Richard Widmark and Janice Rule

programme.title: Congo (1995)
programme.desc: An expedition to find King Solomon's diamond mines in the jungles of the Congo disappears without trace. The tycoon behind the mission sends his assistant, a former CIA agent, to find out what became of the explorers - only to discover that the party met a grisly fate at the hands of a horde of vicious apes. Action adventure based on Michael Crichton's novel, with Dylan Walsh, Laura Linney, Tim Curry and Grant Heslov

programme.title: Commando (1985)
programme.desc: The daughter of retired special forces officer John Matrix is kidnapped by an old comrade-in-arms, now in league with a deposed former dictator. The tyrant plans to use the girl to force Matrix into assassinating the head of the country he once ruled, but is unprepared for the lengths to which the veteran soldier will go to rescue his child. Action adventure, with Arnold Schwarzenegger and Rae Dawn Chong

programme.title: Bone Tomahawk (2015)
programme.desc: After an outlaw unknowingly leads a band of cannibalistic cave-dwellers into the peaceful town of Bright Hope, the elderly sheriff rounds up a posse to rescue townsfolk abducted by the ferocious tribesmen. Western horror, starring Kurt Russell and Patrick Wilson

programme.title: The Quiet Ones (2014)
programme.desc: An academic scientist recruits a group of Oxford students for a strange experiment, performing a series of tests on a woman in the hope of tapping into her apparent psychic abilities and deliberately manifesting a poltergeist. However, they soon find they are unable to control the dark forces they unleash. Horror, starring Jared Harris and Sam Claflin

programme.title: Track of the Cat (1954)
programme.desc: A troubled family starts to fall apart during a harsh winter in 19th-century northern California while staving off the menacing presence of a predator - until a tragic accident finally brings matters to a head. Western melodrama, starring Robert Mitchum, Teresa Wright and Diana Lynn

programme.title: Flying Tigers (1942)
programme.desc: An American pilot leads a squadron of volunteer aviators to help China's fledgling air force fight the Japanese during the Second World War. However, he has to contend with the consequences when the actions of an arrogant hotshot pilot lead to the death of a fellow veteran airman. Drama, starring John Wayne, John Carroll, Anna Lee and Paul Kelly

programme.title: Three Faces West (1940)
programme.desc: An Austrian doctor and his daughter arrive in the dust bowl regions of America after fleeing the Nazis, and join the exodus of farmers seeking fertile land in Oregon. The daughter falls for the pioneer leading the journey, despite being engaged to the man who helped her escape the Third Reich. Drama, starring John Wayne, Charles Coburn and Sigrid Gurie

programme.title: Sink the Bismarck! (1960)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama, charting the mission to track down and eliminate the pride of the German fleet, a fearsome battleship devastating British vessels in the North Atlantic, orchestrated by a British naval officer recovering from his wife's death in an air raid. Starring Kenneth More, Dana Wynter, Laurence Naismith and Karel Stepanek

programme.title: Pitch Perfect 2 (2015)
programme.desc: A Cappella group the Barden Bellas enjoy an unprecedented run of success, until a disastrous performance sees them banned from national competitions and beset by scandal. They attempt to turn their fortunes around and redeem their reputation by competing in the World Championships of A Cappella in Copenhagen. Comedy sequel, starring Anna Kendrick and Rebel Wilson

programme.title: Red Heat (1988)
programme.desc: A Russian detective travels to America to oversee the extradition of a Georgian drug baron responsible for supplying cocaine in Moscow. He is teamed up with a hostile Chicago cop, but when their prisoner kills an officer and escapes, they put aside their mutual mistrust to bring him to justice. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, James Belushi, Peter Boyle, Laurence Fishburne and Ed O'Ross

programme.title: Attack the Block (2011)
programme.desc: A student nurse living in a south London tower block escapes a dangerous encounter with a teenage gang when aliens invade. As the creatures cause mayhem throughout the estate, she joins forces with her would-be attackers to fight off the extraterrestrial forces. Sci-fi comedy thriller, with Jodie Whittaker, John Boyega and Nick Frost

programme.title: Pain & Gain (2013)
programme.desc: Three bodybuilders make a disastrous attempt to get rich by kidnapping a wealthy businessman who goes to their gym and forcing him to sign over his fortune to them. The plan goes awry when the hostage escapes and hires a relentless private detective to track them down. Fact-based action comedy, with Mark Wahlberg, Dwayne Johnson and Anthony Mackie

programme.title: Two by Two (2015)
programme.desc: Two strange creatures and their children are barred from getting on board Noah's Ark, but manage to sneak in. When the kids fall overboard, they face a struggle for survival as the flood waters rise, while their distraught parents must work together to rescue them. Animated adventure, with the voices of Tara Flynn and Dermot Magennis

programme.title: Rise of the Guardians (2012)
programme.desc: Magical beings from folklore, including Santa Claus, Jack Frost and the Easter Bunny, join forces to defeat the plans of an evil spirit, who intends to steal the dreams of children and use the power they grant him to dominate the world. Animated adventure, with the voices of Chris Pine, Alec Baldwin, Hugh Jackman, Isla Fisher and Jude Law

programme.title: A Dog's Purpose (2017)
programme.desc: A dog is reincarnated as different breeds over several generations, with multiple owners and with a variety of names, but he is fated to be reacquainted, decades later, with his original owner. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Quaid, with the voice of Josh Gad

programme.title: Hotel for Dogs (2009)
programme.desc: An orphaned brother and sister face losing their beloved dog when they go into a foster home that does not allow pets. The kids find the ideal hiding place in an abandoned hotel, and set about transforming it into luxury canine accommodation for not just their beloved pet, but every stray in town. Family comedy, starring Emma Roberts, Jake T Austin, Don Cheadle and Lisa Kudrow

programme.title: Non-Stop (2014)
programme.desc: An air marshal on a transatlantic flight receives mysterious messages in which an anonymous terrorist threatens to kill one passenger every 20 minutes unless a ransom is paid. He searches the plane for the criminals responsible, but his investigation is threatened when he is framed as being the mastermind behind the plot. Action thriller, starring Liam Neeson, Julianne Moore and Lupita Nyong'o

programme.title: Last Action Hero (1993)
programme.desc: A boy watching an action movie is transported into the film by a magic ticket and meets its hero figure, who refuses to believe he is a fictional character. Matters take a turn for the worse when the screen villain gets hold of the ticket and crosses over into the real world - where bad guys can win. Comedy adventure, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Austin O'Brien, Charles Dance, F Murray Abraham and Anthony Quinn

programme.title: Climax (2018)
programme.desc: A group of dancers have gathered in a deserted building to rehearse, but someone has spiked the sangria. Writer/director Gaspar Noe's psychological horror, starring Sofia Boutella, Kiddy Smile and Roman Guillermic. In French with English subtitles

programme.title: The Neon Demon (2016)
programme.desc: An aspiring model arrives in Los Angeles, but her beauty and youth soon generate intense fascination and jealousy within the fashion industry. Nicolas Winding Refn's psychological horror, starring Elle Fanning, Jena Malone, Bella Heathcote, Abbey Lee, Christina Hendricks and Keanu Reeves

Comedy adventure, starring Melissa McCarthy, Jason Statham and Rose Byrne

programme.desc: Jo Frost helps a family with six fiercely independent children to bring some order to their lives, in particular, helping the older kids to deal with the death of their father

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Competitive adrenaline junkie Mark wants to fly high on his big day and has an adventurous plan to take his wedding to the sky. However, his straight-talking bride-to-be Lynsey is much more down to earth and has a very clear idea of the modest and intimate wedding she wants. While Mark aims to go above and beyond to make his wedding First Class, no nonsense Lynsey just hopes he is thinking about her first and foremost

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Tom plans a wedding at his favourite theme park, but his down-to-earth fiancee Becca has always dreamed of being treated like a princess on her special day

Beverly helps Erica to get through her post-break-up depression in time for Valentine's Day. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon develops a mental block and has difficulty working on a formula, so in a move inspired by Albert Einstein he gets a job at the Cheesecake Factory diner to try to revive his creativity - and despite Penny's objections, proves surprisingly effective in the role. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is invited to visit the Large Hadron Collider in Switzerland and asks Penny to join him as a Valentine's Day treat. However, Sheldon tries to persuade the couple to allow him to take her place

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon misses an opportunity to meet Spider-Man co-creator Stan Lee when he has to go to court for driving through a red light, and his anger leads to his imprisonment. Penny finds out the address of the comic-book writer and takes Sheldon there - but his troubles with the law have yet to reach their conclusion and the visit does not go as expected

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends fight over a ring they believe was used in the Lord of the Rings film trilogy. Leonard hopes to return the item to its rightful owner - but Raj wishes to sell it so he can buy a jet ski, and Sheldon develops a Gollum-like obsession with the precious object

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is awarded a prestigious prize by the university, but is scared of collecting the gong at a ceremony because he knows he'll have to give a speech. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: The comedy flashes back to reveal how Sheldon saved the lives of his pals, and looks at the origins of his friendship with Leonard seven years previously, as well as explaining the housemates' agreement. Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons star

programme.title: The Simpsons Movie (2007)
programme.desc: Homer unwittingly pollutes Lake Springfield, so the US government decides to isolate the city underneath a giant dome. The Simpsons manage to escape and plan to start a new life in Alaska - but learn Springfield is about to be destroyed, and return to save their friends. With the voices of Dan Castellaneta, Julie Kavner, Nancy Cartwright and Yeardley Smith

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Alex Horne sets another selection of inventively pointless tasks for the celebrity quintet of Daisy May Cooper, Johnny Vegas, Katherine Parkinson, Mawaan Rizwan and Richard Herring. Daisy flirts with a security guard, Richard is surprised by some chickens and Johnny convinces Alex to wear sparkly shoes while operating heavy machinery. As ever, Greg Davies decides who is the best of the worst

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies challenges Hugh Dennis to wield a blade, while Mel Giedroyc destroys a mannequin. Alex Horne helps coax the contestants

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: In the series finale, Noel Fielding finds an unlikely soul mate, Lolly Adefope shows everyone the money, and Mel Giedroyc bites off more than she can chew

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during May 2019, casting their gaze on programmes including What/If, Piers Morgan's Life Stories, Jeremy Wade's Dark Waters, Britain's Got Talent, Beat the Chef and the News. Narrated by Craig Cash

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The regular gaggle of families, friends and viewing party acquaintances share their insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques. Here, they cast their gaze at EastEnders, Britain's Got Talent: The Champions, Peaky Blinders, Expedition with Steve Backshall, Gordon Ramsay: Uncharted and First Dates Hotel. First shown in September

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A 19-year-old computer enthusiast goes on the first date of his life, while a music producer and a singer who both enjoyed pop success in the 1970s meet at Fred's restaurant. Other guests in this episode include a PR executive who always finds one little thing that puts her off every man she meets, and two strangers with a love of dancing

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A 25-year-old man turns up to a date accompanied by his mother, while a former theatre stage manager who has been single since the death of her partner eight years ago meets a fellow Scot who opens up about his own experience of grief. Meanwhile, two Welsh dog-lovers go on a date, and a reiki practitioner meets a writer of supernatural fiction

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Competitive adrenaline junkie Mark wants to fly high on his big day and has an adventurous plan to take his wedding to the sky. However, his straight-talking bride-to-be Lynsey is much more down to earth and has a very clear idea of the modest and intimate wedding she wants. While Mark aims to go above and beyond to make his wedding First Class, no nonsense Lynsey just hopes he is thinking about her first and foremost

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Tom plans a wedding at his favourite theme park, but his down-to-earth fiancee Becca has always dreamed of being treated like a princess on her special day

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Nidderdale agricultural show in the Yorkshire Dales, where she learns how to make her own pork pies and sausages. However, her success jeopardised by problems the evening before the event

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mother and son Martine and Remi from Newport Pagnell are searching for a dream family holiday home along the Charente coast on the island of Oleron in France

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Wales, where they pick up and restore a dentist's table and a barber's chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge visits Philip and Angelina and helps them build a bridge at their chateau - will Dick's design work? Jonathan and Michael test the services they provide at their chateau by inviting Michael's old college mates for the weekend, while Jonny and Ashley continue the search for their dream chateau

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: In West Sussex, Charlie Luxton helps Cathryn and Adam Raybould to build their dream home on Cathryn's family farm. The couple like period houses and hope to give their new build a traditional look. The project has a bumpy start and the whole family must muck in if they're going to finish the job

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Shiralee Bed & Breakfast in Salisbury, run by Fay Lahiani and her partner Ray Cooper, where some guests are shocked by the colourful and cluttered decor, while the presence of CCTV cameras also causes concern

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Bluebell Woods in Northampton, David and Donna Bletsoe Brown try to bring a touch of class to their woodland B&B, but Fay Lahiani and Ray Cooper are on the warpath and are quick to discover a mucky mattress. Meanwhile, Marc Harris and Wayne Williams reveal their competitive streak during an afternoon of horse riding

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Lion Hotel in Pontypool for the third day of the contest, where manager Marc Harris and head chef Wayne Williams aim to deliver a high end service. Although some of the guests are impressed by the rooms, Ray and Fay find it too small for the price, and simmering tensions between some of the competitors leads to a clash over dinner. At breakfast, Wayne's food comes under fire, but it is David and Donna's feedback about finding a nail in their room that really rattles their hosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final guest house of the week is the New Inn, Talgarth, where ex-naval officer Damon Lewis is the sole captain of his ship. Wayne and Marc are determined to be brutally honest, and Donna and David are forced to work closely with their rivals Fay and Ray during an afternoon team-building exercise. Over dinner, Damon admits that taking on the B&B, pub and restaurant all by himself may have not been the best decision - as proved when he has trouble running a solo breakfast service

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time, with Fay and Ray from Shiralee B&B wanting to find out why David and Donna were so critical of their cleanliness. The group members continue to air their grievances as they find out what they've been paid, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Golden poison frog Pablo has lost interest in his partner Maria, so 'bad boy' El Loco is brought in to shake things up. In the chimpanzee habitat, there is a power struggle for the role of dominant female, and baby porcupine Marion fends off the meerkat mob

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge searches for a stash of wine, said to be hidden by the Germans during the Second World War. Fiona starts renovating a dining room for a visit by the mayor. Philip and Angelina continue to prepare their chateau and its grounds for a big event, but they have a lot to do and are running out of time

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: In Canberra, IT engineer Damien Lederer wants to build a family home filled with every sophisticated feature his hi-tech brain can create, from computer-controlled blinds to clear-to-opaque glass, a two-storey helical glass staircase and an air-powered laundry chute. As well as having a house that can think for itself, Damien aims use green building materials and create a zero-carbon home. However, it's not easy to wrap all the latest wizadry and comforts up in a strict green philosophy. Peter Maddison monitors his progress

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tiffany and Jonny Wood build a modernist home in Bath, opting for an environmentally friendly prefabricated German kit house, but first they have to prepare the site - an extremely steep hill. When the couple are close to starting construction, disaster strikes as a storm hits

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruise
programme.desc: Daily life on the mammoth cruise ship the Symphony of the Seas, revealing how some of the 6,680 guests occupy themselves on board this billion-pound vessel that's full of surprises

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Home: Building for a Billionaire
programme.desc: Following the complex build of an extraordinary home costing millions in Mayfair, London, which belongs to self-made billionaire and philanthropist John Caudwell. Having sold Phones 4u in 2006 for just under 1.5 billion pounds, John is now aiming to create one of the most lavish and expensive homes ever to be renovated in Britain. As the build enters the final crucial stages, the programme follows this unique and challenging engineering project as a team of expert trades people and interior designers attempt to pull off one of the most extraordinary renovations in Britain

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at St George's A&E in south-west London tend to 59-year-old Debby, who cut her hand on her lawnmower while distracted by thoughts of her son's forthcoming wedding. Also visiting the emergency room is 70-year-old George, whose eye has been impaled by a piece of driftwood, after he fell while bailing rainwater from his boat moored on the Thames. Plus, eight-year-old Phoebe arrives after accidentally sitting on a pair of scissors she left on a chair - getting them stuck in her thigh

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz as Sean Lock and Henning Wehn compete against Jon Richardson and Victoria Coren Mitchell. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters with 'help' from Joe Wilkinson

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruise
programme.desc: Daily life on the mammoth cruise ship the Symphony of the Seas, revealing how some of the 6,680 guests occupy themselves on board this billion-pound vessel that's full of surprises

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman who suffers from nocturnal epileptic fits brings in Pearl, a Staffordshire bull terrier that can sense when these episodes are about to happen. However, the dog has problems with its knees and struggles to walk, so the pressure is on for Noel Fitzpatrick to restore it back to full health. Plus, an Irish setter has a cancerous tumour in its front leg, and a cat needs its jaw wiring back together after being hit by a car

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Building the Dream

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: It's crunch time for Jonathan and Michael who are facing their investors, but their newest idea - to keep goats - does not go down well. Philip and Angelina host the big anniversary party they've been spending weeks preparing for, but with broken toilets and 60 guests to contend with, how will it go? Plus, single mother Fiona battles to get her dining room ready to show off to the mayor

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: In the bush ranger territory of the Strathbogie Ranges, Peter Maddison meets Eddie and Dot, who dream of a home with a magnificent 360-degree view. However, they need to work around huge granite boulders to achieve their dream

programme.title: Grand Designs Revisited
programme.desc: Checking on the progress of David and Greta Iredale, who replaced the timber house they designed themselves more than 40 years ago with a Huf Haus that was constructed by an efficient German construction team in just six days. Kevin McCloud finds out how successful the pair were in personalising the property

programme.title: The Same Sky
programme.desc: Lauren is deeply torn between her feelings for Lars and responsibility towards her wayward son Emil and is heading towards a crisis. Juliane notices serious changes in Klara's physical appearance and confronts her. Duncan returns from the West bearing gifts for Axel, who is suddenly acutely aware of a once in a lifetime opportunity. Detective thriller, starring Tom Schilling, Sofia Helin and Friederike Becht. In German and English

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary charting the work of staff at St George's A&E continues, this time focusing on the stories of patients whose friendships have seen them through good times and bad. Phil, a 60-year-old bricklayer, is rushed in after suffering multiple injuries following a short fall from a ladder at work, and his business partner Paul discusses how the pair bonded over the course of 28 years. Meanwhile, 93-year-old widow Violet arrives after fracturing her leg while preparing to go out for lunch with the friends who have supported her since the death of her husband, and 18-year-old dance student Shiana comes to A&E with her mother and her best friend, after injuring her ankle during a rehearsal

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow couples as they cope with treatment, including Corey, who's 27. He is rushed to St George's after suffering a cardiac arrest during a game of ice hockey, and his wife of seven days, Alice, was watching him play when he collapsed. There's also a profile of John, 55. He fell 10 feet while taking down scaffolding at home, and his spouse Penny talks about how he has supported her through sickness and bereavement during their marriage

programme.title: Ramsay's Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Ramsay continues his battle to restore the failing Town's Inn in Harpers Ferry, West Virginia. After witnessing abysmal food hygiene practices in the restaurant, the chef is left in disbelief as he uncovers terrifying cleanliness issues throughout the hotel. Can he win over stubborn owner Karan Townsend and persuade her to turn things round?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel tries to fix a cocker spaniel's leg problems by fitting two bionic back feet, before launching a series of investigations into what is causing a cat to drag its leg. Plus, a Westie puppy with a fractured knee arrives, but it cannot keep still

programme.title: Grand Designs

They decide to hire an unconventional architect and give him free rein to create whatever kind of house he sees fit. However, he designs an experimental building that provokes scepticism

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This competition comes from in and around Newcastle, where first up is 25-year-old barista Delphinium, who's eager to prove that vegetarians can win with her travel-inspired menu. The guests she's hoping to impress this week are 64-year-old financial advisor David, art teacher Karen, development manager Mark and 34-year-old mobile hairdresser Deborah. It's an unusual evening complete with strange food, shocking name reveals and dessert taking an unexpected turn

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Fussy eater Debs is unexcited at the prospect of goat's cheese or salmon en croute and is more concerned whether Karen has made her own pastry, and while vegetarian Delphinium is underwhelmed by Karen's menu, she really gets stuck into Karen's art class entertainment. Software salesman Mark winds Debs up about 64-year-old ladies' man David, who likes to shock the group with his antics in Ibiza

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Newcastle, mobile hairdresser and amateur musical songstress Debbie plans to bring showbiz razzle dazzle to her night. Humble pie from Mark shocks Karen, and there are very unusual dumplings for barista Delph and Ibiza-loving David to try

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: The trio of motoring enthusiasts embark on another epic road trip, this time across western America in front-engined supercars. Jeremy Clarkson pilots the Lexus LFA, which has a spectacularly high-revving V10 powerplant, Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the muscular Dodge Viper, and James May opts for the latest incarnation of Aston Martin's Vanquish. Meanwhile, Louis, the chef onboard Anjodi, rustles up seafood with squid ink pasta

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a race from London's Wembley Stadium to Milan's San Siro, the home of Italian football giants AC and Inter. A price restriction leads Jeremy to grab the keys to a Shelby Mustang GT500, which costs the equivalent of £35,000 in America and packs a supercharged punch of more than 650bhp. James and Richard pin their hopes on the pan-European rail network in a bid to secure the prize of tickets to a top-flight football match, with nothing but a long journey home awaiting the losers. On the test track, Jeremy puts Toyota's GT86 coupe through its paces, and its promise of entertaining rear-wheel-drive handling should put a smile on his face. Plus, singer-songwriter Amy Macdonald gets to grips with the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Kia Cee'd through a rigorous but peculiar road test featuring eels, curry, a rock legend, an American police officer and an unusual game of rugby involving James May. Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the Mastretta MXT, but worries about the repercussions of a comment he made in a previous episode about Mexico, the sports car's country of manufacture. Plus, three high-performance hatchbacks are driven with gusto around the test track, and Formula One driver Lewis Hamilton gets a second chance to smash the lap record

programme.title: Big Zuu's Big Eats
programme.desc: Grime artist and chef Big Zuu takes his food van to Manchester where Ed Gamble is performing. Ed's love of fried chicken and all things cheese-related gives Zuu plenty to work with

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: In the company of comedian Rob Delaney, Richard Ayoade packs his carry-on case and sets off to enjoy an optimal two-day mini city break in Seville, southern Spain. On arrival, the duo soon learn to dance flamenco and eat tapas and churros. They also explore Metropol Parasol, one of the boldest pieces of modern architecture in Europe and visit a film set built for the spaghetti western A Fistful of Dollars. After seeking to channel their inner Eastwood, the amigos repair to the saloon for some Fanta and a Kinder egg

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Sara Pascoe, news anchor Clive Myrie, and TV presenters Angela Scanlon and Rick Edwards continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills. Hosted by Richard Osman

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon introduces a seasonal special of the comedy panel game. Screen hardman Ray Winstone, comic Josh Widdicombe, maths whizz Rachel Riley and actor Ricky Tomlinson join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with a few festive fibs or facts about themselves. Does one of the contestants really apologise to their boiled egg before smashing its head in? Who apparently writes Christmas cards with their feet? And did one of the line-up really prevent their bandmate joining the Beatles?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which two teams of celebs try to call each other's bluff with a few stories about themselves that might be true or might be out-and-out porkies. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are EastEnders star June Brown, comedians Aisling Bea and Seann Walsh, and football presenter Adrian Chiles

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef journeys along the Etang de Thau, famous for its oysters and mussels, before mooring his barge in the port of Sete, where Bastille Day is being celebrated. He also rustles up seafood ragout and roast black bream before travelling on to Marseille to tackle the most famous fish dish of all - bouillabaisse

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef's epic journey from the Atlantic to the Mediterranean comes to an end as he takes the final leg of his gastronomic tour along the canal systems of southern France. Arriving in Marseille, he samples seafood and regional cuisine, as well as showing a variety of traditional recipes. He also cooks roast black bream and finds time to send Chalky a postcard

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May immerse themselves in nostalgia and try to prove that hot hatches from their younger days are better than today's equivalents. Jeremy grabs the keys to a Volkswagen Golf GTI, Richard takes the wheel of a Vauxhall Nova SRi and James May opts for the Ford Fiesta XR2i, and the trio set off on a road trip that includes a hill climb and a visit to a supermarket. However, their adventure concludes at an army training ground in Wales, where they and their steeds must face the heavily armed Top Gear Police Department. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville blasts the Reasonably Priced Car around the test track

programme.title: Big Zuu's Big Eats
programme.desc: Big Zuu and his sidekicks Tubsey and Hyder travel to the spa town of Bath to sample the waters and cook for comedian and vegan Lou Sanders

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by Jo Brand for a two-day spell in the Italian city. The pair stay at the famous Hotel Danieli, formerly the lodgings of Lord Byron, Charles Dickens and Indiana Jones, and in almost no time learn to row a gondola, take in the famous sights, sample the Venetian version of tapas, and explore the world of masks. They also road-test Whaiwhai - a book used with a map and mobile that takes people on a treasure hunt around the city - only without the treasure

programme.desc: Betrayed, Stalin fights the German army in the Soviet states. The German occupation policies lead to the first extermination policies of 'undesirables'

programme.desc: Stories of people who came close to losing their lives during the Second World War, beginning with tales about the Chindits, the army's elite jungle troops division who beat the Japanese in a daring mission

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A seemingly endless concrete line stretching through the French countryside, which sits as a monument to a discarded engineering advancement

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his trusty copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, Michael Portillo explores the former domain of the Habsburg monarchs, beginning in the Hungarian capital Budapest. He then travels to Austria via Bratislava in Slovakia, and in Vienna he immerses himself in pre-First World War decadence. From there he heads to Salzburg, before going on to the Salzkammergut region to visit the emperor's Austrian summer residence at Bad Ischl, where in 1914 Franz Joseph I signed a declaration that changed the course of European history

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a Christmas special from Lyme Park in Cheshire, where she and toy expert Will Farmer look back at children's Christmas presents dating back to Edwardian times. In the house's gardens, over 2000 visitors have arrived to have their items valued, including many with a royal heritage. The programme features a cup and saucer once owned by the Queen, a pram from Buckingham Palace, and a dagger believed to have been owned by Charles I. Plus, a celebration of the works of Charles Dickens

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: On the Welsh Highland Railway, trainee Claire Oates takes her driving test, while chef Alex and manager Michelle prepare a dinner for 130 people on the East Lancashire railway

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: An insight into the construction of the cutting-edge Ford class aircraft carriers, the largest warships ever built, which are almost as long as the Empire State Building is tall

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A little-known dark past explains the vast monolithic structures that dominate the small British island of Guernsey

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The bravery of the 333rd Field Artillery Battalion, a depleted unit of African-American soldiers who risked everything to fight off the Nazis at Bastogne, Belgium

programme.title: Adolf Hitler's War
programme.desc: As Hitler's troops advance, occupants in Ukraine, Belarus and Poland take the brunt of the occupying forces cruelty; and the first concentration camps are created

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Germany, beginning his journey in Berlin - which at the beginning of the 20th Century was a powerhouse of science and technology. Led by his copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, he heads west via the Harz Mountains to the industrial Ruhr Valley to learn how imperial Germany was ready for war. He then goes south along the tourist trail of the castle-studded Rhine river and ends in the Rheingau to taste the wines of its vineyards

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Bowood House in Wiltshire. Among the items being brought in for valuation are a collection of glass ornaments, an 18th-century portrait that children have used for target practice, a carved walking stick, a book documenting the travels of a 19th-century explorer and an account of the sinking of the Titanic by an officer who survived

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Following in the footsteps of early 20th-century travellers, Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to explore Switzerland, which was a favourite with Edwardian tourists. He begins in Basel, travels east to visit the industrial regions of Zurich and learns about the engineering feats needed to thread the rail network through the Alps. He takes in the striking beauty of Lake Lucerne, before ending with an ascent by train to Europe's highest station at Jungfraujoch

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to examine more items brought in by viewers, beginning with a visit to Plas Newydd Country House and Gardens on Anglesey. Collectibles assessed include a Victorian cane carved with an image of the Menai suspension bridge, an 18th century grandmother clock, and two giant toy giraffes

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek takes a shine to a rare 1958 Armstrong Siddeley Sapphire with a long family history and a convertible Triumph Herald brings a few smiles on auction day

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: At St Athan in Wales, engineer John Sparks and his team of ex-Navy and RAF engineers begin the task of reassembling the Czech-built jet from nine parts delivered by lorry

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Projects include a maze of tunnels filled with haunting remains beneath the streets of Paris and a strange formation of domed structures hovering over the waters off Florida

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of Major General Robert Laycock who rallied his ill-equipped troops to see off a German attack on Crete, Greece

programme.title: Nicholas and Alexandra
programme.desc: Part one of two. Suzannah Lipscomb presents a profile of the last Russian tsar and empress, beginning with a look at their unconventional courtship and religious upbringings

programme.title: Nicholas and Alexandra
programme.desc: Part two of two. Suzannah Lipscomb examines the lives of the royal couple during the revolution and their eventual execution, and considers how history has come to view them

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: The historian reveals a story of invasion in Britain spanning millennia, beginning with him felling a tree with a flint axe in Kent, and riding an Iron Age chariot. Sam Willis also searches for clues of invasion at Silbury Hill, and tracks down evidence of the Beaker people, who brought ceramics, metalwork and beakers to Britain

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: Sam Willis tells the story of the Barbary Corsaire pirates who made their headquarters on Lundy Island. He also sheds light on the little-known tale of King Louis the Lion, a French King who invaded Britain in the 13th century

programme.title: Sings the Beatles
programme.desc: A look back through the BBC's archives at memorable covers of classic Beatles songs. Included are a performance of Day Tripper by Sandie Shaw, Shirley Bassey's version of Something, the Carpenters' rendition of Help!, Oasis's reinvention of I Am the Walrus, and Joe Cocker's chart-topping interpretation of With a Little Help from My Friends. Plus, Paul McCartney's solo performance of Lady Madonna

programme.title: The Rolling Stones at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of the band's performances from the BBC archives, in a programme originally broadcast in 2012 to celebrate their 50th anniversary. Songs include The Last Time, Let's Spend The Night Together, Get Off of My Cloud, Honky Tonk Women, Gimme Shelter, Miss You and Start Me Up. Plus, footage of the band performing 19th Nervous Breakdown on Top of the Pops in 1966, which was used in a 1960s BBC documentary about women with depression

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the length and breadth of the country with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and find out what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins in Liverpool by learning to speak Scouse, before researching the first railway fatality and exploring the origins of the Eccles cake

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The second leg of Michael Portillo's journey from Liverpool to Scarborough takes him from Manchester to Bury. Along the way, he finds out about the life of Victorian train enthusiast George Bradshaw, gets fitted for a trilby hat in Denton, and learns how railways helped contribute to the nation's love affair with fish and chips

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey, travelling on the Embsay and Bolton Abbey steam railway, finding out about Roman discoveries in York, and taking to the air in the Network Rail helicopter

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey inspired by George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook. He searches for the last liquorice grower in Pontefract, finds out how the railways transformed Hull into one of the largest white-fish ports in the world, and goes fishing for sea bass in Bridlington

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In East Yorkshire, Michael Portillo goes bird-watching on the cliffs of Flamborough Head before travelling to Filey in North Yorkshire, where he learns to decipher traditional knitting patterns. He then continues his journey to the Victorian seaside resort of Scarborough, and views a 4,000-year-old skeleton called Gristhorpe Man

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the first leg of his second journey, Michael Portillo heads to Kirkcaldy in Fife, finding out along the way how the mill town of Preston in Lancashire gave birth to the Temperance Movement. In Blackpool, he enjoys a performance in the Tower Ballroom and discovers the railways had a direct impact on the town's birth as a resort. After a stroll along the pier with local comedian Joey Blower, Michael visits Morecambe Bay

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Martyn Colbeck and Cynthia Moss examine the legacy of Echo the elephant, who died in 2009 after being followed on film during the final chapter of her life, having guided her family for half a century. With a drought devastating the animals' home in the shadow of Kilimanjaro, a 38-strong band of relatives and descendants must overcome the loss of their leader, hunger and the threat of poachers to survive. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: Extended programme exploring the world's only sloth orphanage in Costa Rica, and meeting the characters that live there, including Alfie, who suffers from stunted growth

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the different methods animals use to attract a mate, revealing how inventive, loving and complex they can be. A female polar bear tries to revive a male's interest in her by turning cartwheels in front of him, while a bird of paradise tidies obsessively as he prepares a stage for his dance. While other lemurs are fighting for the right to breed, one sidles up to a female and wins favour by wafting his scent at her, and silverback gorilla seduction involves staring with a fixed grin

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the most difficult cases he has taken on since the series began. First, he returns to Cafe Hon in Baltimore, Maryland, where owner Denise Whiting was trapped in a nasty battle with the community and had threatened the locals with lawsuits. Next, the chef goes back to Chiarella's in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. When he first visited owners Tommy and Dina DeFino, the business was struggling so badly that Tommy had completely lost his confidence and wife Dina had lost her patience. On his final stop, Gordon travels to Leone's in Montclair, New Jersey, where Rose Leone was depending on her lazy son Michael to run the restaurant

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lecturer Maxine and paramedic team leader Cormac got married in the scenic northern region of the Dordogne in France, and now want Ben Hillman's help to find them their dream holiday home there. They have a budget of just over £100,000 and would ideally like a property with lots of outside space, just a short walk from the town of Riberac.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton help a couple to choose between a property on the western coastline of Wales and a home in Carcassonne in the south of France

programme.title: Heston's Feasts
programme.desc: Heston Blumenthal decides to recreate the sensual thrills of 19th-century gothic horror novels. He makes a blood risotto starter and an edible Frankenstein's monster, before treating his guests to a Marquis de Sade-inspired dessert in the form of their own graves. Diners include Ulrika Jonsson, Nicholas Parsons and Colin Murray

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back contributors whose differences were the making, or breaking, of their dates, including pharmacist Peter and German shoe manufacturer Gregor. Elsewhere, 26-year-old Nathan may have made a name for himself on the Watford club scene, but could he get on the right track with businesswoman Jade?

programme.title: Taskmaster

Mawaan Rizwan attempts an upside-down farm-related illusion, Daisy May Cooper softens some pasta and Katherine Parkinson lays waste to a garden arch. With Johnny Vegas and Richard Herring

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: Educating Greater Manchester
programme.desc: The documentary series following life at Harrop Fold School returns three years on, featuring a new generation of year sevens who already have a reputation among staff members for mischief. The new series also catches up with youngsters from the 2017 series, who are now moving into year 10. The school is on high alert after a member of the public calls in, claiming to have seen a pupil with a knife, forcing Mr Povey and his staff to instigate bag searches

programme.title: Educating Greater Manchester
programme.desc: With GCSEs looming for the current head boy and girl, the search begins for new candidates, and surprise applicant Holly talks candidly about her disability as she makes her bid for the position. Meanwhile, Mr Povey tackles the mental wellbeing of his pupils by arranging for a group of therapy animals to come into the school to help address issues such as anxiety and ADHD

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The team wakes up to the realisation that more than half of the bridge still needs building - and that they will likely lose a team member too. With rumours spreading through the camp that a splinter group are plotting to vote someone out, it soon transpires that the whispers are part of an intricately calculated game plan to crown a new leader

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: There is nasty shock as the group members wake up to discover that former team-mate Luke has sabotaged their progress in exchange for £10,000 from the prize fund. Zac finds it hard to believe that his best friend would do such a thing and considers throwing in the towel, while newly elected leader Rowan is in the firing line. In the most testing of final days, can the team pull together, rebuild the bridge and reach the money?

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Return of the programme following elite bakers and sugarcraft specialists, with Suzanne making a detailed cake replica of Lloyd's of London's Lutine Bell

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Promotions representative Dawn Griffiths hosts the first dinner party in Middlesbrough, and has confidence in her Asian-themed evening of curry and traditional Indian dancing. However, her rowdy guests - practical joker Ron Gordon, market trader Kay Griffiths, teacher Karena Serdecka and refuse collector Paul Tidy - prove hard to please

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Practical joker Ron Gordon hosts the second party in Middlesbrough, but despite his expertly cooked food, his hopes of an evening of elegance are placed in jeopardy by market trader Kay Griffiths, who dominated proceedings the previous night, but irritated her fellow diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Self-confessed loudmouth Kay Griffiths hosts the third dinner party in Middlesbrough, hoping to liven up her guests with good food, colourful conversation, a little conflict and her home-made punch

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drama teacher Karena Serdecka hosts the fourth dinner party in Middlesbrough, treating her guests to a Dutch and Polish menu, before taking to the stage to sing with her band

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Refuse collector Paul Tidy hosts the final evening in Middlesbrough, throwing a film star-themed party in the hope of impressing his guests. His plans take a turn for the worse when one of his diners expresses a dislike of garlic, which happens to be one of the chef's favourite ingredients, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies draws on her property expertise to help find Neil and Linda from Lanarkshire a rustic holiday home around the picturesque town of Antequera in southern Spain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a Yorkshire couple with six children and foster children to find a holiday home in Mojacar, southern Spain, on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up for a final showdown, as Kaye and Lily bring Harriet and Richard's blind issues to light before dishing the dirt on Sophie and David's discoveries. Amanda wants to know why frank feelings were unleased when it came to her canine companion, Trudy. David and Sophie banish Harriet and Richard's bathroom blues, before confronting Kaye and Lily with their cleanliness critique. programme.title: The Great House Giveaway
programme.desc: Strangers Harry and Kaylum buy a majestic detached house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Greater Manchester at auction - then have six months to renovate and sell it on for a profit. Can they put their differences aside and turn this property around?

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray buys a new vacuum cleaner that is intended to make housework easier for the family, but instead causes friction between Marie and Debra

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, where he meets a stubborn owner who doesn't react well to criticism. Can the chef break Tom's iron grip on the business and persuade him to change his outdated approach?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off

programme.desc: Documentary following the reopening of the theme park after a long period of closure due to Covid-19. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third day of the competition in Plymouth. Former sailor Tony Wade thinks of himself as the best cook in the group, and prepares a range of complicated dishes to prove it. He also hopes to ease relations between Annie Wroe and Suzanne Hyde by sitting them next to each other, which is a risky undertaking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment manager Sarah Joy hosts the penultimate dinner party in Plymouth. She hopes to create a happier atmosphere after a series of disagreements, but outspoken guest Suzanne has other ideas. Later, an after-dinner disco gives Charlie the opportunity to perform a Scottish jig

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former Navy soldier Charlie Brunel hosts the final dinner party in Plymouth, and plans to get his guests into the party mood by organising a pirate-themed evening, complete with fancy dress and a fish dish, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Choir teacher Dionne hosts the first of five parties from in and around Leeds, hoping her menu inspired by family favourites will impress Billy, Jane, Darren and Denver

programme.desc: Finance director Jane hosts the second night in Leeds. Her plan to grab the grand is to treat her guests to a night of fine dining, but she has her work cut out. Car painter Darren is concerned that Jane's menu of home-made ravioli, pork and panna cotta could win the week, but he's less impressed when it gets to the table. programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: An actor pours his savings into revamping his two-bedroomed home in Plaistow, east London, in the hope of making a killing on the property market

programme.title: Royal Cousins at War
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines the key role played by royalty in smoothing the path to the Anglo-Russian entente of 1907, and the part played by the Kaiser's erratic, unstable personality in the growing isolation of Germany in the years leading up to 1914. The film also explores the roles played by the German, British and Russian monarchs in the months leading up to the outbreak of the First World War and tells the story of George V's refusal to grant his cousin Nicholas II asylum in England following the Russian Revolution of 1917

programme.title: Killing Hitler
programme.desc: Part one of two. A blend of documentary, dramatic reconstruction and expert military analysis brings to life the blueprint of Operation Foxley - a 1944 plan to assassinate Adolf Hitler. Featuring testimonies from operatives of Churchill's Special Operations Executive and one of the Fuehrer's most trusted bodyguards, the ethical right to kill an enemy head of state is debated during the unfolding story of LB/X - the man in charge of the plot to kill the Allies' most hated foe

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: The Big Blockade (1942)
programme.desc: Second World War mock-documentary, featuring a series of scenes about the importance of Britain's economic blockade on Germany, demonstrating the international repercussions of the action and the responses of the British public. Starring Leslie Banks, Michael Redgrave, John Mills, Will Hay and Robert Morley

programme.title: Date with a Dream (1948)
programme.desc: Four wartime performers well known for their rousing concert recitals decide to reform and become a successful nightclub act all over again. Upbeat comedy, starring Wally Patch, Len Lowe, Terry-Thomas, Norman Wisdom and Jean Carson

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb comes to the rescue of Peter Sullivan and Sally Turner, who are searching for an affordable holiday home on the Caribbean island of Barbados. Among the choices are a new apartment for £66,000 and a traditional villa

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: British couple Dave and Sue Lewington weigh up their options as they trawl Florida's Sun Coast, from Tampa to Sarasota, in search of a home for less than £120,000. Presenter Amanda Lamb offers them expert guidance

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: I Was There: The Great War Interviews
programme.desc: In the early 1960s, the BBC spoke to almost 300 people about their experiences during the First World War for landmark series The Great War, but only a fraction of the interviews were broadcast. This programme airs a selection of the original material, most of which has never been screened before, with recollections of the horrors of artillery bombardment and fleeting outbreaks of peace on the battlefield, as well as the stories of those on the home front waiting for news of their loved ones. Narrated by Samuel West

programme.title: Killing Hitler
programme.desc: Conclusion of the investigation into Operation Foxley - the 1944 plan to assassinate Adolf Hitler - blending documentary, dramatic reconstruction and expert military analysis to bring to life the blueprint of the audacious plot. Featuring testimonies from members of Churchill's Special Operations Executive and one of the Fuhrer's most trusted bodyguards

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey spreads her wings for the first time since her husband was put behind bars, and invites a handsome footballer round for dinner. Meanwhile, Sharon discovers the joys of literature. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Steve accidentally slices off his finger while using a circular saw, causing all-out panic in the shop, while Mike's girlfriend Anne does the unthinkable and goes shopping at a DIY superstore. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman, Ken Morley, Ryan Cartwright, Peter Serafinowicz and Susan Earl

programme.title: Dead Ringers (1988)
programme.desc: Identical twin gynaecologists Elliot and Beverly share everything in their life. Elliot, self-assured and experienced, passes his unwitting sexual conquests to shy and sensitive Beverly when he tires of them. However, when Beverly falls in love for the first time with a patient, the affair drives the brothers apart - and both men begin to lose their grip on their sanity. David Cronenberg's psychological thriller, with Jeremy Irons in a dual role alongside Genevieve Bujold

programme.title: We Still Steal the Old Way (2017)
programme.desc: After their exploits in We Still Kill the Old Way, criminal outfit the Archer Gang encounters an old enemy when a daring heist ends with them behind bars, however that doesn't stop them attempting to break out an old friend. Prison drama, starring Ian Ogilvy, Billy Murray, Julian Glover and Lysette Anthony

programme.title: Brothers' Day (2015)
programme.desc: A Manchester teenager feels abandoned by his older brother, who has turned to a life of crime. However, when his latest deal ends badly, the younger sibling finds his eyes opened to the impossible choices his brother faces every day. Crime drama, starring Tom Collins and Lewis Fletcher

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists arrive in Austria's capital, Vienna, and play a game of animal charades, followed by a trip to the world's oldest zoo. During lunch, Emma lends the chef a helping hand and the group later unites for a spin on the big wheel

programme.desc: In the early 1960s, the BBC spoke to almost 300 people about their experiences during the First World War for landmark series The Great War, but only a fraction of the interviews were broadcast.
